rules.threat: SSRF in the application reaches 169.254.169.254 and steals the instance-profile credentials when IMDSv1 is allowed.
rules.title: EC2 instance must enforce IMDSv2 with hop limit 1
requiredActions: Add metadata_options { http_tokens = "required", http_put_response_hop_limit = 1 } to the aws_instance so IMDSv2 session tokens are mandatory and the metadata endpoint is unreachable from a single-hop SSRF.

rules.threat: Autoscaling instances launched from a template without IMDSv2 expose instance-profile credentials to SSRF.
rules.title: Launch template must enforce IMDSv2
requiredActions: Add metadata_options { http_tokens = "required", http_put_response_hop_limit = 1 } to the aws_launch_template.

rules.threat: An unencrypted EBS volume or its snapshot, if exfiltrated or shared, exposes data at rest with no key barrier.
rules.title: EBS volume must be encrypted at rest
requiredActions: Set encrypted = true on the aws_ebs_volume and supply a customer-managed kms_key_id.

rules.threat: A publicly accessible RDS instance is directly reachable from the internet for brute force and CVE exploitation.
rules.title: RDS instance must not be publicly accessible
requiredActions: Set publicly_accessible = false on the aws_db_instance and place it in private subnets behind a security group that only allows the application tier.

rules.threat: Unencrypted RDS storage exposes the database contents if the underlying volume or a snapshot is accessed.
rules.title: RDS storage must be encrypted
requiredActions: Set storage_encrypted = true (and kms_key_id) on the aws_db_instance. Note: encryption can only be enabled at creation; rebuild from an encrypted snapshot if the instance already exists.

rules.threat: A public-read ACL lets anonymous users list and download every object in the bucket.
rules.title: S3 bucket must not use a public ACL
requiredActions: Set acl = "private" and attach an aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block with all four block flags = true.

rules.threat: Without a public access block, a future bucket policy or ACL can silently expose the bucket to the internet.
rules.title: S3 bucket must have a public access block

remediate.companion:
resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "${name}" {
  bucket                  = aws_s3_bucket.${name}.id
  block_public_acls       = true
  block_public_policy     = true
  ignore_public_acls      = true
  restrict_public_buckets = true
}

requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block referencing this bucket with block_public_acls, block_public_policy, ignore_public_acls, and restrict_public_buckets all set to true.

rules.threat: A customer-managed KMS key without rotation means a single key compromise exposes all historical ciphertext.
rules.title: KMS key must have automatic rotation enabled
requiredActions: Set enable_key_rotation = true on the aws_kms_key.

rules.threat: A single-region trail misses global service events (IAM, STS, CloudFront); an attacker operating in another region is unlogged.
rules.title: CloudTrail must be multi-region
requiredActions: Set is_multi_region_trail = true on the aws_cloudtrail so global and cross-region API activity is captured.

rules.threat: Without log file validation an attacker can tamper with or delete trail objects to cover tracks undetected.
rules.title: CloudTrail must enable log file validation
requiredActions: Set enable_log_file_validation = true on the aws_cloudtrail.

rules.threat: A Lambda function URL with authorization_type NONE is an unauthenticated internet endpoint that can reach the function's IAM role and downstream data stores.
rules.title: Lambda function URL must require IAM auth
requiredActions: Set authorization_type = "AWS_IAM" on the aws_lambda_function_url, or front it with API Gateway / a Lambda authorizer that enforces authentication.

rules.threat: Without point-in-time recovery, a destructive write or ransomware action against the table is unrecoverable.
rules.title: DynamoDB table must enable point-in-time recovery
requiredActions: Add point_in_time_recovery { enabled = true } to the aws_dynamodb_table.

rules.threat: A security group rule allowing 0.0.0.0/0 exposes admin/database ports directly to internet-wide scanning and brute force.
rules.title: Security group rule must not open ports to 0.0.0.0/0

remediate.snippet: Replace cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"] with the specific corporate / VPC CIDR ranges that require access. For SSH/RDP prefer SSM Session Manager or a bastion.

requiredActions: Restrict cidr_blocks to known source ranges. Auto-fix cannot choose a safe replacement CIDR — review and set the correct allowlist.

rules.threat: Auto-assigning a public IP places the instance directly on the internet, widening the attack surface beyond the load balancer.
rules.title: EC2 instance must not auto-assign a public IP
requiredActions: Set associate_public_ip_address = false and reach the instance through a load balancer, NAT, or SSM Session Manager.

rules.threat: Without versioning, an accidental or malicious overwrite/delete of objects is unrecoverable (no ransomware rollback).
rules.title: S3 bucket should enable versioning
requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_versioning resource for this bucket with versioning_configuration { status = "Enabled" }.

rules.threat: Images pushed without vulnerability scanning can ship known-exploitable CVEs straight into production.
rules.title: ECR repository must scan images on push
requiredActions: Add image_scanning_configuration { scan_on_push = true } to the aws_ecr_repository.

rules.threat: Mutable tags let an attacker who gains push access overwrite a trusted tag (e.g. :latest) with a backdoored image.
rules.title: ECR repository must use immutable tags
requiredActions: Set image_tag_mutability = "IMMUTABLE" on the aws_ecr_repository.

rules.threat: An unencrypted EFS file system exposes shared data at rest with no key barrier.
rules.title: EFS file system must be encrypted
requiredActions: Set encrypted = true (and kms_key_id) on the aws_efs_file_system.

rules.threat: An unencrypted SNS topic stores and forwards potentially sensitive messages without encryption at rest.
rules.title: SNS topic must be encrypted at rest
requiredActions: Set kms_master_key_id (a CMK alias is preferred over alias/aws/sns) on the aws_sns_topic.

rules.threat: An unencrypted SQS queue holds in-flight messages — often containing PII or tokens — without encryption at rest.
rules.title: SQS queue must enable server-side encryption
requiredActions: Set sqs_managed_sse_enabled = true (or kms_master_key_id for a CMK) on the aws_sqs_queue.

rules.threat: A log group with infinite retention silently accumulates sensitive data forever; one with no retention set defaults to never expire, raising breach blast radius.
rules.title: CloudWatch log group must set a retention period
requiredActions: Set retention_in_days on the aws_cloudwatch_log_group (365 is a common compliance baseline) and add kms_key_id for encryption.

rules.threat: A publicly accessible Redshift cluster is directly reachable from the internet for brute force and exploitation.
rules.title: Redshift cluster must not be publicly accessible
requiredActions: Set publicly_accessible = false on the aws_redshift_cluster and place it in private subnets.

rules.threat: An unencrypted Redshift cluster exposes the data warehouse contents if storage or a snapshot is accessed.
rules.title: Redshift cluster must be encrypted
requiredActions: Set encrypted = true (and kms_key_id) on the aws_redshift_cluster.

rules.threat: Without dropping invalid HTTP headers, an ALB can forward request-smuggling payloads to backends.
rules.title: ALB must drop invalid HTTP headers
requiredActions: Set drop_invalid_header_fields = true on the application aws_lb.

rules.threat: An unencrypted DocumentDB cluster exposes document data at rest if storage or a snapshot is accessed.
rules.title: DocumentDB cluster must be encrypted
requiredActions: Set storage_encrypted = true (and kms_key_id) on the aws_docdb_cluster.

rules.threat: An ElastiCache replication group without at-rest encryption exposes cached data — often session tokens or PII — if storage is accessed.
rules.title: ElastiCache replication group must encrypt at rest
requiredActions: Set at_rest_encryption_enabled = true (and transit_encryption_enabled = true) on the aws_elasticache_replication_group.

rules.threat: A SageMaker notebook with direct internet access is an exfiltration and command-and-control channel from within the VPC.
rules.title: SageMaker notebook must disable direct internet access
requiredActions: Set direct_internet_access = "Disabled" on the aws_sagemaker_notebook_instance and route egress through a VPC NAT with controls.

rules.threat: Without account-default EBS encryption, any volume created without an explicit flag lands unencrypted.
rules.title: Account default EBS encryption must be enabled
requiredActions: Set enabled = true on aws_ebs_encryption_by_default so all new EBS volumes are encrypted by default.

rules.threat: Without deletion protection, a compromised credential or accidental apply can destroy the production database.
rules.title: RDS instance must enable deletion protection
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true on the aws_db_instance.

rules.threat: Disabling automatic minor version upgrades leaves the database running known-vulnerable engine patch levels.
rules.title: RDS instance must allow automatic minor version upgrades
requiredActions: Set auto_minor_version_upgrade = true on the aws_db_instance.

rules.threat: A zero/short backup retention window means no point-in-time recovery after a destructive or ransomware event.
rules.title: RDS instance must set a backup retention period
requiredActions: Set backup_retention_period to at least 7 (days) on the aws_db_instance.

rules.threat: An unencrypted Neptune cluster exposes graph data at rest if storage or a snapshot is accessed.
rules.title: Neptune cluster must be encrypted
requiredActions: Set storage_encrypted = true (and kms_key_arn) on the aws_neptune_cluster.

rules.threat: A disabled GuardDuty detector means no managed threat detection on the account — credential theft and recon go unseen.
rules.title: GuardDuty detector must be enabled
requiredActions: Set enable = true on the aws_guardduty_detector.

rules.threat: An OpenSearch domain without at-rest encryption exposes indexed data if the underlying storage is accessed.
rules.title: OpenSearch domain must encrypt at rest
requiredActions: Add encrypt_at_rest { enabled = true } to the aws_opensearch_domain.

rules.threat: Without node-to-node encryption, intra-cluster OpenSearch traffic is plaintext and sniffable inside the VPC.
rules.title: OpenSearch domain must use node-to-node encryption
requiredActions: Add node_to_node_encryption { enabled = true } to the aws_opensearch_domain.

rules.threat: An OpenSearch domain that does not enforce HTTPS accepts plaintext queries carrying credentials and data.
rules.title: OpenSearch domain must enforce HTTPS
requiredActions: Add domain_endpoint_options { enforce_https = true } to the aws_opensearch_domain.

rules.threat: A DMS replication instance defaults to publicly accessible, exposing the migration pipeline to the internet.
rules.title: DMS replication instance must not be publicly accessible
requiredActions: Set publicly_accessible = false on the aws_dms_replication_instance (it defaults to true).

rules.threat: Without X-Ray tracing, attack reconnaissance and abuse against API Gateway stages is harder to detect and investigate.
rules.title: API Gateway stage should enable X-Ray tracing
requiredActions: Set xray_tracing_enabled = true on the aws_api_gateway_stage.

rules.threat: Without deletion protection, a load balancer fronting production can be destroyed by a compromised credential, causing an outage.
rules.title: Load balancer should enable deletion protection
requiredActions: Set enable_deletion_protection = true on the aws_lb.

rules.threat: A bucket without an explicit server-side encryption configuration relies on account defaults that may not use a CMK.
rules.title: S3 bucket should declare server-side encryption
requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration for this bucket using aws:kms (or AES256).

rules.threat: Without access logging, bulk object reads (data exfiltration) leave no forensic trail.
rules.title: S3 bucket should enable access logging

remediate.snippet: Add an aws_s3_bucket_logging resource pointing at a dedicated log-archive bucket (target_bucket). Auto-fix cannot choose the log destination.

requiredActions: Add an aws_s3_bucket_logging resource for this bucket. Choose a separate, access-restricted target_bucket for the logs.

rules.threat: An EKS cluster API server exposed publicly without a private endpoint is reachable for credential stuffing and CVE exploitation.
rules.title: EKS cluster should enable a private API endpoint

remediate.snippet: In vpc_config set endpoint_private_access = true and restrict public_access_cidrs to your admin ranges (or disable endpoint_public_access). Networking choices require review.

requiredActions: Enable endpoint_private_access and restrict public_access_cidrs on the aws_eks_cluster vpc_config.

rules.threat: A privileged ECS task container has host-level capabilities — a container escape becomes a host compromise.
rules.title: ECS task definition must not run privileged containers

remediate.snippet: Remove "privileged": true from the container_definitions JSON and grant only the specific Linux capabilities required.

requiredActions: Set privileged to false (or remove it) in the container_definitions. Auto-fix does not edit embedded JSON container definitions.

rules.threat: A secret without automated rotation stays valid indefinitely if leaked, maximising the window of a credential compromise.
rules.title: Secrets Manager secret should have rotation configured

remediate.snippet: Add an aws_secretsmanager_secret_rotation resource with a rotation_lambda_arn and rotation_rules { automatically_after_days = 30 }. The rotation Lambda must be supplied.

requiredActions: Configure automatic rotation for this secret via aws_secretsmanager_secret_rotation.

rules.threat: An unencrypted Aurora cluster exposes the database at rest if storage or a snapshot is accessed.
rules.title: Aurora/RDS cluster must be encrypted
requiredActions: Set storage_encrypted = true (and kms_key_id) on the aws_rds_cluster.

rules.threat: Without deletion protection, a compromised credential can destroy the entire Aurora cluster.
rules.title: Aurora/RDS cluster must enable deletion protection
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true on the aws_rds_cluster.

rules.threat: A short/zero cluster backup window means no point-in-time recovery after a destructive event.
rules.title: Aurora/RDS cluster must set a backup retention period
requiredActions: Set backup_retention_period to at least 7 on the aws_rds_cluster.

rules.threat: A publicly accessible Aurora cluster instance is directly reachable from the internet.
rules.title: Aurora cluster instance must not be publicly accessible
requiredActions: Set publicly_accessible = false on the aws_rds_cluster_instance and place it in private subnets.

rules.threat: Without in-transit encryption, ElastiCache traffic — often session tokens — is plaintext on the wire.
rules.title: ElastiCache replication group must encrypt in transit
requiredActions: Set transit_encryption_enabled = true on the aws_elasticache_replication_group.

rules.threat: An ECR repository without an explicit KMS encryption configuration relies on default keys that may not be customer-managed.
rules.title: ECR repository should declare KMS encryption
requiredActions: Add encryption_configuration { encryption_type = "KMS" } (and kms_key) to the aws_ecr_repository.

rules.threat: An unencrypted Kinesis stream stores records in plaintext at rest.
rules.title: Kinesis stream must enable server-side encryption
requiredActions: Set encryption_type = "KMS" (and kms_key_id) on the aws_kinesis_stream.

rules.threat: Without X-Ray active tracing, abuse and exploitation of a Lambda is harder to detect and trace.
rules.title: Lambda function should enable active tracing
requiredActions: Add tracing_config { mode = "Active" } to the aws_lambda_function.

rules.threat: Without VPC Flow Logs there is no network-level record of exfiltration or lateral movement.
rules.title: VPC should enable flow logs

remediate.snippet: Add an aws_flow_log for this VPC writing to CloudWatch Logs or S3. Auto-fix cannot choose the log destination / IAM role.

requiredActions: Add an aws_flow_log resource for this aws_vpc with a log destination and IAM role.

rules.threat: A CloudFront distribution without a WAF web ACL has no managed protection against L7 attacks (injection, bots, DDoS).
rules.title: CloudFront distribution should attach a WAF web ACL

remediate.snippet: Set web_acl_id to an aws_wafv2_web_acl (scope = CLOUDFRONT) ARN. The WAF rule set must be designed for the app.

requiredActions: Attach a WAFv2 web ACL via web_acl_id on the aws_cloudfront_distribution.

rules.threat: A CodeBuild project without a CMK encrypts artifacts/logs with default keys that may be broadly accessible.
rules.title: CodeBuild project should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Set encryption_key to a customer-managed aws_kms_key ARN on the aws_codebuild_project.

requiredActions: Set encryption_key to a CMK ARN on the aws_codebuild_project.

rules.threat: A short minimum password length makes console credentials trivially brute-forceable.
rules.title: IAM password policy must require length >= 14
requiredActions: Set minimum_password_length = 14 (or higher) on the aws_iam_account_password_policy.

rules.threat: Without password-reuse prevention, users can cycle back to a known-compromised password.
rules.title: IAM password policy must prevent password reuse
requiredActions: Set password_reuse_prevention = 24 on the aws_iam_account_password_policy.

rules.threat: An unencrypted DAX cluster caches DynamoDB data — often PII — in plaintext at rest.
rules.title: DAX cluster must encrypt at rest
requiredActions: Add server_side_encryption { enabled = true } to the aws_dax_cluster.

rules.threat: An ECR repository policy allowing Principal "*" lets any AWS account pull (or push) images.
rules.title: ECR repository policy must not allow any principal

remediate.snippet: Replace Principal { AWS = "*" } with the specific account/role ARNs allowed to pull. Auto-fix cannot infer the correct principals.

requiredActions: Scope the aws_ecr_repository_policy to explicit account/role principals.

rules.threat: An SNS topic policy with Principal "*" and no source condition lets any account publish or subscribe.
rules.title: SNS topic policy must not allow any principal

remediate.snippet: Add an aws:SourceOwner / aws:SourceArn condition or scope the Principal. Auto-fix cannot infer the correct condition.

requiredActions: Scope the aws_sns_topic_policy Principal or add a source-account condition.

rules.threat: An SQS queue policy with Principal "*" and no source condition lets any account read or send messages.
rules.title: SQS queue policy must not allow any principal

remediate.snippet: Add an aws:SourceArn / aws:SourceAccount condition or scope the Principal on the aws_sqs_queue_policy.

requiredActions: Scope the aws_sqs_queue_policy Principal or add a source condition.

rules.threat: A publicly shared EBS snapshot lets any AWS account restore a full copy of the volume's data.
rules.title: EBS snapshot must not be publicly shared

remediate.snippet: Remove the 'all' account_id from aws_snapshot_create_volume_permission and share only with specific account IDs.

requiredActions: Restrict snapshot sharing to specific account IDs; never 'all'.

rules.threat: Without EMR account block-public-access, a cluster security group can expose Hadoop/Spark UIs to the internet.
rules.title: EMR block public access must be enabled
requiredActions: Set block_public_security_group_rules = true on the aws_emr_block_public_access_configuration.

rules.threat: A publicly accessible Amazon MQ broker is reachable from the internet for brute force and protocol abuse.
rules.title: Amazon MQ broker must not be publicly accessible
requiredActions: Set publicly_accessible = false on the aws_mq_broker and place it in private subnets.

rules.threat: A MemoryDB cluster without TLS sends cache traffic — often tokens — in plaintext on the wire.
rules.title: MemoryDB cluster must enable TLS
requiredActions: Set tls_enabled = true on the aws_memorydb_cluster.

rules.threat: Without enforced workgroup configuration, clients can override the encryption/output settings and write unencrypted query results.
rules.title: Athena workgroup must enforce its configuration
requiredActions: Set configuration { enforce_workgroup_configuration = true } on the aws_athena_workgroup (and configure result encryption).

rules.threat: Without enhanced VPC routing, Redshift COPY/UNLOAD traffic leaves the VPC and bypasses VPC-level egress controls.
rules.title: Redshift cluster should use enhanced VPC routing
requiredActions: Set enhanced_vpc_routing = true on the aws_redshift_cluster.

rules.threat: A short Neptune backup window means no point-in-time recovery after a destructive event.
rules.title: Neptune cluster must set a backup retention period
requiredActions: Set backup_retention_period to at least 7 on the aws_neptune_cluster.

rules.threat: A short DocumentDB backup window means no point-in-time recovery after a destructive event.
rules.title: DocumentDB cluster must set a backup retention period
requiredActions: Set backup_retention_period to at least 7 on the aws_docdb_cluster.

rules.threat: A legacy Elasticsearch domain without at-rest encryption exposes indexed data if storage is accessed.
rules.title: Elasticsearch domain must encrypt at rest
requiredActions: Add encrypt_at_rest { enabled = true } to the aws_elasticsearch_domain (migrate to aws_opensearch_domain where possible).

rules.threat: An Amazon MQ broker without a customer-managed key encrypts messages with default keys that may be broadly accessible.
rules.title: Amazon MQ broker should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Add encryption_options { kms_key_id = <cmk-arn>, use_aws_owned_key = false } to the aws_mq_broker.

requiredActions: Configure encryption_options with a CMK on the aws_mq_broker.

rules.threat: A CloudFront distribution without access logging leaves no record of abuse, scraping, or attack traffic.
rules.title: CloudFront distribution should enable access logging

remediate.snippet: Add logging_config { bucket = <log-bucket>.s3.amazonaws.com, include_cookies = false } to the aws_cloudfront_distribution. Choose a dedicated log bucket.

requiredActions: Add a logging_config block pointing at a dedicated log bucket on the aws_cloudfront_distribution.

rules.threat: Root access on a SageMaker notebook lets a user (or attacker who lands in it) install kernels/agents and tamper with the instance.
rules.title: SageMaker notebook must disable root access
requiredActions: Set root_access = "Disabled" on the aws_sagemaker_notebook_instance.

rules.threat: An unencrypted WorkSpaces volume exposes user data at rest if the underlying storage is accessed.
rules.title: WorkSpaces volumes must be encrypted
requiredActions: Set user_volume_encryption_enabled = true and root_volume_encryption_enabled = true on the aws_workspaces_workspace.

rules.threat: An ECS service assigning public IPs to tasks places workloads directly on the internet, widening the attack surface.
rules.title: ECS service must not assign public IPs to tasks
requiredActions: Set network_configuration { assign_public_ip = "DISABLED" } on the aws_ecs_service and use private subnets with NAT.

rules.threat: A log group without a KMS key stores potentially sensitive log data unencrypted with broad default access.
rules.title: CloudWatch log group should be encrypted with a CMK

remediate.snippet: Set kms_key_id to a customer-managed aws_kms_key ARN on the aws_cloudwatch_log_group (the key policy must allow the logs service).

requiredActions: Set kms_key_id (CMK) on the aws_cloudwatch_log_group.

rules.threat: A secret encrypted with the default key is readable by anyone with broad KMS access rather than a scoped CMK policy.
rules.title: Secrets Manager secret should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Set kms_key_id to a customer-managed aws_kms_key ARN on the aws_secretsmanager_secret with a tightly scoped key policy.

requiredActions: Set kms_key_id (CMK) on the aws_secretsmanager_secret.

rules.threat: Without control-plane logging, API server audit events (auth, RBAC, admission) are not recorded for detection or forensics.
rules.title: EKS cluster should enable control plane logging

remediate.snippet: Set enabled_cluster_log_types = ["api","audit","authenticator","controllerManager","scheduler"] on the aws_eks_cluster.

requiredActions: Enable enabled_cluster_log_types (at least api and audit) on the aws_eks_cluster.

rules.threat: An HTTP load balancer listener that is not a redirect serves application traffic in plaintext.
rules.title: Load balancer listener should use HTTPS

remediate.snippet: Use protocol = "HTTPS" with an ACM certificate, OR keep the HTTP listener only as a redirect (default_action type = "redirect" to HTTPS). Auto-fix cannot tell a redirect listener from a plaintext one.

requiredActions: Serve traffic over HTTPS; keep any HTTP listener as a redirect-only to HTTPS.

rules.threat: An API Gateway method with authorization NONE is an unauthenticated endpoint — only acceptable for intentionally public routes.
rules.title: API Gateway method should require authorization

remediate.snippet: Set authorization to AWS_IAM / a Cognito or Lambda authorizer, or document the route as intentionally public with api_key_required = true. Auto-fix cannot decide the auth model.

requiredActions: Require authorization on the aws_api_gateway_method unless the route is intentionally public.

rules.threat: Disabling QLDB deletion protection lets a compromised credential destroy the immutable ledger and its audit history.
rules.title: QLDB ledger must keep deletion protection
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true on the aws_qldb_ledger.

rules.threat: An EFS file system without automatic backups cannot be restored after a destructive or ransomware event.
rules.title: EFS file system should enable automatic backups
requiredActions: Set backup_policy { status = "ENABLED" } on the aws_efs_backup_policy.

rules.threat: A publicly accessible Neptune cluster instance is directly reachable from the internet.
rules.title: Neptune cluster instance must not be publicly accessible
requiredActions: Set publicly_accessible = false on the aws_neptune_cluster_instance.

rules.threat: Without deletion protection, a compromised credential can destroy the entire DocumentDB cluster.
rules.title: DocumentDB cluster must enable deletion protection
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true on the aws_docdb_cluster.

rules.threat: A CloudTrail without a CMK encrypts logs with default keys, weakening protection of the forensic record.
rules.title: CloudTrail should be encrypted with a CMK

remediate.snippet: Set kms_key_id to a customer-managed aws_kms_key ARN on the aws_cloudtrail (key policy must allow CloudTrail).

requiredActions: Set kms_key_id (CMK) on the aws_cloudtrail.

rules.threat: Without CloudWatch Logs integration, CloudTrail events are not available for real-time alerting on suspicious API activity.
rules.title: CloudTrail should deliver to CloudWatch Logs

remediate.snippet: Set cloud_watch_logs_group_arn and cloud_watch_logs_role_arn on the aws_cloudtrail to enable metric-filter alerting.

requiredActions: Integrate the aws_cloudtrail with a CloudWatch Logs group for alerting.

rules.threat: An MSK cluster allowing plaintext broker traffic exposes Kafka messages to interception inside the VPC.
rules.title: MSK cluster must encrypt data in transit

remediate.snippet: Add encryption_info { encryption_in_transit { client_broker = "TLS", in_cluster = true } } to the aws_msk_cluster.

requiredActions: Set encryption_in_transit client_broker = TLS on the aws_msk_cluster.

rules.threat: An AppSync GraphQL API without logging leaves abuse, introspection, and injection attempts unrecorded.
rules.title: AppSync GraphQL API should enable logging

remediate.snippet: Add log_config { field_log_level = "ERROR", cloudwatch_logs_role_arn = <role> } to the aws_appsync_graphql_api.

requiredActions: Add a log_config block to the aws_appsync_graphql_api.

rules.threat: If account-level Block Public Access is partly disabled, any bucket ACL or policy can re-expose data to the internet.
rules.title: Account-level S3 Block Public Access must be fully enabled
requiredActions: Set all four block flags = true on aws_s3_account_public_access_block.

rules.threat: A CloudFront behavior allowing plain HTTP serves content and cookies over an interceptable channel.
rules.title: CloudFront must redirect viewers to HTTPS
requiredActions: Set viewer_protocol_policy = "redirect-to-https" (or "https-only") on every cache behavior of the aws_cloudfront_distribution.

rules.threat: Disabling automatic minor upgrades leaves the cache engine on known-vulnerable patch levels.
rules.title: ElastiCache must allow automatic minor version upgrades
requiredActions: Set auto_minor_version_upgrade = true on the aws_elasticache_replication_group.

rules.threat: Without copy-tags-to-snapshot, access-control and ownership tags are lost on snapshots, weakening least-privilege on restores.
rules.title: RDS instance should copy tags to snapshots
requiredActions: Set copy_tags_to_snapshot = true on the aws_db_instance.

rules.threat: Without enhanced monitoring, OS-level metrics needed to detect resource-exhaustion attacks on the DB host are missing.
rules.title: RDS instance should enable enhanced monitoring

remediate.snippet: Set monitoring_interval = 60 and monitoring_role_arn = <role> on the aws_db_instance. The monitoring IAM role must be supplied.

requiredActions: Enable enhanced monitoring (monitoring_interval + monitoring_role_arn) on the aws_db_instance.

rules.threat: A task sharing the host PID namespace can see and signal host processes — a container escape aid.
rules.title: ECS task must not share the host PID namespace

remediate.snippet: Remove pid_mode = "host" from the aws_ecs_task_definition (use "task" or omit).

requiredActions: Do not set pid_mode = host on the aws_ecs_task_definition.

rules.threat: Without audit logging, malicious queries and data exfiltration from Redshift leave no trail.
rules.title: Redshift cluster should enable audit logging

remediate.snippet: Add a logging { enable = true, bucket_name = <log-bucket> } block (or aws_redshift_logging) to the aws_redshift_cluster.

requiredActions: Enable audit logging on the aws_redshift_cluster to a dedicated log bucket.

rules.threat: Disabling Redshift version upgrades keeps the cluster on known-vulnerable engine versions.
rules.title: Redshift cluster must allow version upgrades
requiredActions: Set allow_version_upgrade = true on the aws_redshift_cluster.

rules.threat: Without delete protection, a compromised credential can tear down the Network Firewall and open all egress/ingress.
rules.title: Network Firewall must enable delete protection
requiredActions: Set delete_protection = true on the aws_networkfirewall_firewall.

rules.threat: Disabling cross-zone load balancing concentrates traffic and can be abused to overload a single AZ's targets.
rules.title: Load balancer should keep cross-zone balancing enabled
requiredActions: Set enable_cross_zone_load_balancing = true on the aws_lb (NLB).

rules.threat: A legacy Elasticsearch domain without node-to-node encryption sends intra-cluster traffic in plaintext.
rules.title: Elasticsearch domain must use node-to-node encryption
requiredActions: Add node_to_node_encryption { enabled = true } to the aws_elasticsearch_domain.

rules.threat: A legacy Elasticsearch domain that does not enforce HTTPS accepts plaintext queries carrying credentials.
rules.title: Elasticsearch domain must enforce HTTPS
requiredActions: Add domain_endpoint_options { enforce_https = true } to the aws_elasticsearch_domain.

rules.threat: Without envelope encryption, Kubernetes Secrets are stored only base64-encoded in EKS etcd.
rules.title: EKS cluster should encrypt Kubernetes secrets with KMS

remediate.snippet: Add encryption_config { resources = ["secrets"], provider { key_arn = <cmk> } } to the aws_eks_cluster. The KMS key must be supplied.

requiredActions: Enable secrets envelope encryption (encryption_config) on the aws_eks_cluster.

rules.threat: Without CloudWatch log exports, DB engine/audit/error logs aren't available for detection of injection or abuse.
rules.title: RDS instance should export logs to CloudWatch

remediate.snippet: Set enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports = ["audit","error","general","slowquery"] (engine-specific log types) on the aws_db_instance.

requiredActions: Enable enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports on the aws_db_instance (log types vary by engine).

rules.threat: Host network mode gives the task the host's network stack, bypassing per-task SG isolation.
rules.title: ECS task should not use host network mode

remediate.snippet: Use network_mode = "awsvpc" (per-task ENI + SG) instead of "host" on the aws_ecs_task_definition.

requiredActions: Switch the aws_ecs_task_definition off host network mode (prefer awsvpc).

rules.threat: Instances launched from a launch configuration without IMDSv2 expose instance-profile credentials to SSRF.
rules.title: Launch configuration must enforce IMDSv2
requiredActions: Add metadata_options { http_tokens = "required", http_put_response_hop_limit = 1 } to the aws_launch_configuration (or migrate to aws_launch_template).

rules.threat: Auto-assigning public IPs to autoscaled instances places workloads directly on the internet.
rules.title: Launch configuration must not auto-assign public IPs
requiredActions: Set associate_public_ip_address = false on the aws_launch_configuration.

rules.threat: A Redis replication group without snapshot retention cannot be restored after a destructive event.
rules.title: ElastiCache (Redis) should retain automatic snapshots
requiredActions: Set snapshot_retention_limit to at least 1 (e.g. 7) on the aws_elasticache_replication_group.

rules.threat: Permissive desync mitigation lets HTTP request-smuggling payloads pass through the load balancer to backends.
rules.title: Load balancer must not use monitor-only desync mitigation
requiredActions: Set desync_mitigation_mode = "defensive" (or "strictest") on the aws_lb.

rules.threat: Password-only Aurora auth lacks centralized, short-lived IAM credentials; a leaked DB password is standing access.
rules.title: Aurora cluster should enable IAM database authentication

remediate.snippet: Set iam_database_authentication_enabled = true on the aws_rds_cluster — confirm clients use IAM auth tokens before enabling (it changes how apps connect).

requiredActions: Enable IAM database authentication on the aws_rds_cluster where supported.

rules.threat: Without IAM database authentication, Neptune access relies on weaker, longer-lived mechanisms.
rules.title: Neptune cluster should enable IAM database authentication

remediate.snippet: Set iam_database_authentication_enabled = true on the aws_neptune_cluster — confirm clients use IAM auth first.

requiredActions: Enable IAM database authentication on the aws_neptune_cluster.

rules.threat: Without code signing, tampered or supply-chain-poisoned deployment packages run unchecked.
rules.title: Lambda function should enforce code signing

remediate.snippet: Create an aws_lambda_code_signing_config (trusted signing profile) and set code_signing_config_arn on the aws_lambda_function.

requiredActions: Attach a code_signing_config_arn to the aws_lambda_function.

rules.threat: Without stage access logging, API abuse, scraping, and injection attempts go unrecorded.
rules.title: API Gateway stage should enable access logging

remediate.snippet: Add access_log_settings { destination_arn = <log-group>, format = ... } to the aws_api_gateway_stage.

requiredActions: Enable access_log_settings on the aws_api_gateway_stage.

rules.threat: Without IAM database authentication, the database relies solely on long-lived static passwords that, once leaked, grant standing access with no central revocation.
rules.title: RDS instance should enable IAM database authentication
requiredActions: Set iam_database_authentication_enabled = true on the aws_db_instance so connections use short-lived IAM tokens instead of static passwords.

rules.threat: The default security group is attached implicitly to resources that omit one; an open 0.0.0.0/0 rule on it silently exposes those resources to the internet.
rules.title: Default security group must not allow open traffic

remediate.snippet: Remove all ingress/egress rules from aws_default_security_group (leave the blocks empty) so it denies all traffic; never reference 0.0.0.0/0 on it.

requiredActions: Strip the open rules from the aws_default_security_group; the default SG should permit no traffic and no resource should rely on it.

rules.threat: An HTTPS listener bound to a TLS 1.0/1.1 security policy lets a network attacker force a downgrade to weak ciphers and decrypt or tamper with traffic.
rules.title: Load balancer listener must not use a TLS 1.0/1.1 policy

remediate.snippet: Set ssl_policy to a modern TLS 1.2+ policy such as "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06" on the aws_lb_listener.

requiredActions: Replace the TLS-1-0/TLS-1-1 ssl_policy with a TLS 1.2+ policy (e.g. ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06).

rules.threat: A CloudFront distribution that accepts viewer connections below TLS 1.2 is exposed to protocol-downgrade attacks against clients.
rules.title: CloudFront distribution must require TLS 1.2 minimum

remediate.snippet: Set viewer_certificate { minimum_protocol_version = "TLSv1.2_2021" } on the aws_cloudfront_distribution (only effective with a custom ACM certificate, not the default CloudFront cert).

requiredActions: Set minimum_protocol_version = "TLSv1.2_2021" in viewer_certificate on the aws_cloudfront_distribution.

rules.threat: A backup vault without a customer-managed KMS key encrypts recovery points with default keys, so anyone with default-key access can read the backups that exist precisely to survive a breach.
rules.title: Backup vault must use a customer-managed KMS key

remediate.snippet: Set kms_key_arn to a customer-managed aws_kms_key ARN on the aws_backup_vault.

requiredActions: Set kms_key_arn to a CMK ARN on the aws_backup_vault.

rules.threat: An HTTP API (v2) stage without access logging leaves API abuse, scraping, and injection attempts with no forensic trail.
rules.title: API Gateway v2 stage should enable access logging

remediate.snippet: Add access_log_settings { destination_arn = <log-group>, format = ... } to the aws_apigatewayv2_stage.

requiredActions: Enable access_log_settings on the aws_apigatewayv2_stage.

rules.threat: An ElastiCache replication group with transit encryption but no auth token accepts any in-VPC client; a lateral-movement foothold can read all cached session data without credentials.
rules.title: ElastiCache replication group should require an auth token

remediate.snippet: Set auth_token (a strong secret sourced from Secrets Manager) on the aws_elasticache_replication_group; requires transit_encryption_enabled = true.

requiredActions: Set auth_token on the aws_elasticache_replication_group, sourced from a secret store. Auto-fix cannot generate the token.

rules.threat: A SageMaker endpoint configuration without a KMS key leaves the inference instance's attached storage — which holds model artifacts and buffered request data — encrypted only with default keys.
rules.title: SageMaker endpoint config should use a customer-managed KMS key

remediate.snippet: Set kms_key_arn to a customer-managed aws_kms_key ARN on the aws_sagemaker_endpoint_configuration.

requiredActions: Set kms_key_arn to a CMK ARN on the aws_sagemaker_endpoint_configuration.

rules.threat: An S3 bucket policy with Principal "*" and no condition grants anonymous read/write to every object — the classic public-bucket data breach.
rules.title: S3 bucket policy must not grant access to any principal

remediate.snippet: Replace Principal "*" with explicit account/role ARNs, or add a Condition (e.g. aws:SourceVpce / aws:PrincipalOrgID). Auto-fix cannot infer the correct principals.

requiredActions: Scope the aws_s3_bucket_policy to explicit principals or add a restricting condition; never a bare Principal "*".

rules.threat: A KMS key policy allowing Principal AWS "*" lets any account that can reach the key decrypt data it protects, collapsing the encryption boundary.
rules.title: KMS key policy must not allow any principal

remediate.snippet: Replace Principal { AWS = "*" } in the key policy with the specific account root / role ARNs that need the key. Auto-fix cannot infer the correct principals.

requiredActions: Scope the aws_kms_key policy to explicit principals; never a bare AWS "*".

rules.threat: An aws_lambda_permission with principal "*" and no source restriction lets any caller invoke the function, reaching its IAM role and downstream data.
rules.title: Lambda permission must not grant invoke to any principal

remediate.snippet: Set principal to the specific service or account ARN, and add source_arn / source_account to bound who can invoke. Auto-fix cannot infer the correct caller.

requiredActions: Scope the aws_lambda_permission principal (and add source_arn/source_account); never principal "*" without a source condition.

rules.threat: An IAM policy statement granting Action "*" (effectively admin) means any principal it attaches to can escalate to full account control if compromised.
rules.title: IAM policy must not grant all actions

remediate.snippet: Replace Action "*" with the least-privilege list of specific actions the workload needs. Auto-fix cannot infer the required actions.

requiredActions: Scope the aws_iam_policy to specific actions; never Action "*" (admin-equivalent).

rules.threat: A Secrets Manager resource policy with Principal AWS "*" lets any account read the secret value — credentials, keys, tokens — directly.
rules.title: Secrets Manager resource policy must not allow any principal

remediate.snippet: Replace Principal { AWS = "*" } with the specific account/role ARNs allowed to read the secret. Auto-fix cannot infer the correct principals.

requiredActions: Scope the aws_secretsmanager_secret_policy to explicit principals; never a bare AWS "*".

rules.threat: A load balancer without access logging leaves L7 attacks — scraping, injection, credential stuffing — with no request-level forensic trail.
rules.title: Load balancer should enable access logging

remediate.snippet: Add access_logs { bucket = <log-bucket>, enabled = true } to the aws_lb pointing at a dedicated, access-restricted S3 bucket.

requiredActions: Enable access_logs on the aws_lb targeting a dedicated log bucket.

rules.threat: An EKS cluster with a public API endpoint exposes the Kubernetes control plane to internet-wide credential stuffing and CVE exploitation.
rules.title: EKS cluster must not expose a public API endpoint

remediate.snippet: In vpc_config set endpoint_public_access = false (with endpoint_private_access = true), or restrict public_access_cidrs to admin ranges. Networking choices require review.

requiredActions: Disable endpoint_public_access (or tightly scope public_access_cidrs) on the aws_eks_cluster vpc_config.

rules.threat: A Neptune cluster without audit log exports gives no record of who queried or mutated the graph, blinding incident response.
rules.title: Neptune cluster should export audit logs to CloudWatch
requiredActions: Set enable_cloudwatch_logs_exports = ["audit"] on the aws_neptune_cluster.

rules.threat: A DocumentDB cluster without audit/profiler log exports gives no record of database access, blinding incident response.
rules.title: DocumentDB cluster should export audit logs to CloudWatch
requiredActions: Set enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports = ["audit"] on the aws_docdb_cluster.

rules.threat: An API Gateway stage cache holding unencrypted responses can leak cached PII or tokens if the cache storage is accessed.
rules.title: API Gateway method cache must be encrypted

remediate.snippet: In the settings block of the aws_api_gateway_method_settings set cache_data_encrypted = true (only relevant when caching_enabled = true).

requiredActions: Set cache_data_encrypted = true in the aws_api_gateway_method_settings when response caching is enabled.

rules.threat: An EFS file system policy with Principal AWS "*" lets any account that can reach the mount targets read or write the shared file system.
rules.title: EFS file system policy must not allow any principal

remediate.snippet: Replace Principal { AWS = "*" } with specific account/role ARNs on the aws_efs_file_system_policy. Auto-fix cannot infer the correct principals.

requiredActions: Scope the aws_efs_file_system_policy to explicit principals; never a bare AWS "*".

rules.threat: An RDS Proxy that does not require TLS accepts plaintext client connections, exposing database credentials and queries on the wire.
rules.title: RDS Proxy must require TLS
requiredActions: Set require_tls = true on the aws_db_proxy.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation S3 bucket with a public-read ACL exposes all objects to anonymous download.
rules.title: CloudFormation S3 bucket must not use a public ACL

remediate.snippet: Remove AccessControl: PublicRead/PublicReadWrite and add a PublicAccessBlockConfiguration with all four flags = true to the AWS::S3::Bucket.

requiredActions: Set a private ACL and a PublicAccessBlockConfiguration on the AWS::S3::Bucket.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation RDS instance marked publicly accessible is reachable from the internet.
rules.title: CloudFormation RDS instance must not be publicly accessible

remediate.snippet: Set PubliclyAccessible: false on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance and place it in private subnets.

requiredActions: Set PubliclyAccessible: false on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation RDS instance without storage encryption exposes the database at rest.
rules.title: CloudFormation RDS instance must encrypt storage

remediate.snippet: Set StorageEncrypted: true (and KmsKeyId) on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

requiredActions: Set StorageEncrypted: true on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation EBS volume without encryption exposes data at rest if exfiltrated.
rules.title: CloudFormation EBS volume must be encrypted

remediate.snippet: Set Encrypted: true (and KmsKeyId) on the AWS::EC2::Volume.

requiredActions: Set Encrypted: true on the AWS::EC2::Volume.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation security group allowing 0.0.0.0/0 exposes ports directly to the internet.
rules.title: CloudFormation security group must not open ingress to 0.0.0.0/0

remediate.snippet: Restrict CidrIp from 0.0.0.0/0 to specific ranges on the AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup ingress rules.

requiredActions: Remove 0.0.0.0/0 ingress from the AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation Redshift cluster marked publicly accessible is reachable from the internet.
rules.title: CloudFormation Redshift cluster must not be publicly accessible

remediate.snippet: Set PubliclyAccessible: false on the AWS::Redshift::Cluster.

requiredActions: Set PubliclyAccessible: false on the AWS::Redshift::Cluster.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation Lambda URL with AuthType NONE is an unauthenticated internet endpoint.
rules.title: CloudFormation Lambda URL must require IAM auth

remediate.snippet: Set AuthType: AWS_IAM on the AWS::Lambda::Url, or front it with an authenticated gateway.

requiredActions: Set AuthType: AWS_IAM on the AWS::Lambda::Url.

rules.threat: Without PublicAccessBlockConfiguration a bucket policy or ACL can re-expose objects to the internet.
rules.title: CloudFormation S3 bucket must set a public access block

remediate.snippet: Add PublicAccessBlockConfiguration with all four flags = true to the AWS::S3::Bucket.

requiredActions: Add PublicAccessBlockConfiguration with all four flags = true to the AWS::S3::Bucket.

rules.threat: Without versioning, an overwrite or delete is unrecoverable (no ransomware rollback).
rules.title: CloudFormation S3 bucket should enable versioning

remediate.snippet: Add VersioningConfiguration: { Status: Enabled } to the AWS::S3::Bucket.

requiredActions: Add VersioningConfiguration: { Status: Enabled } to the AWS::S3::Bucket.

rules.threat: A bucket without BucketEncryption relies on account defaults that may not use a CMK.
rules.title: CloudFormation S3 bucket should declare encryption

remediate.snippet: Add a BucketEncryption block (aws:kms) to the AWS::S3::Bucket.

requiredActions: Add a BucketEncryption block (aws:kms) to the AWS::S3::Bucket.

rules.threat: A CMK without rotation means one key compromise exposes all historical ciphertext.
rules.title: CloudFormation KMS key must enable rotation

remediate.snippet: Set EnableKeyRotation: true on the AWS::KMS::Key.

requiredActions: Set EnableKeyRotation: true on the AWS::KMS::Key.

rules.threat: Without point-in-time recovery a destructive write is unrecoverable.
rules.title: CloudFormation DynamoDB table should enable PITR

remediate.snippet: Add PointInTimeRecoverySpecification: { PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true } to the table.

requiredActions: Add PointInTimeRecoverySpecification: { PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true } to the table.

rules.threat: A public IP places the instance directly on the internet, widening the attack surface.
rules.title: CloudFormation EC2 instance must not auto-assign a public IP

remediate.snippet: Set AssociatePublicIpAddress: false in the instance NetworkInterfaces.

requiredActions: Set AssociatePublicIpAddress: false in the instance NetworkInterfaces.

rules.threat: Without IMDSv2, SSRF can steal instance-profile credentials from the metadata endpoint.
rules.title: CloudFormation launch template must enforce IMDSv2

remediate.snippet: Set MetadataOptions: { HttpTokens: required, HttpPutResponseHopLimit: 1 } in LaunchTemplateData.

requiredActions: Set MetadataOptions: { HttpTokens: required, HttpPutResponseHopLimit: 1 } in LaunchTemplateData.

rules.threat: Images pushed without scanning can ship known-exploitable CVEs to production.
rules.title: CloudFormation ECR repository must scan on push

remediate.snippet: Set ImageScanningConfiguration: { ScanOnPush: true } on the AWS::ECR::Repository.

requiredActions: Set ImageScanningConfiguration: { ScanOnPush: true } on the AWS::ECR::Repository.

rules.threat: Mutable tags let an attacker overwrite a trusted tag with a backdoored image.
rules.title: CloudFormation ECR repository must use immutable tags

remediate.snippet: Set ImageTagMutability: IMMUTABLE on the AWS::ECR::Repository.

requiredActions: Set ImageTagMutability: IMMUTABLE on the AWS::ECR::Repository.

rules.threat: An unencrypted EFS exposes shared data at rest.
rules.title: CloudFormation EFS file system must be encrypted

remediate.snippet: Set Encrypted: true on the AWS::EFS::FileSystem.

requiredActions: Set Encrypted: true on the AWS::EFS::FileSystem.

rules.threat: An unencrypted SNS topic stores messages without encryption at rest.
rules.title: CloudFormation SNS topic must be encrypted at rest

remediate.snippet: Set KmsMasterKeyId on the AWS::SNS::Topic.

requiredActions: Set KmsMasterKeyId on the AWS::SNS::Topic.

rules.threat: An unencrypted SQS queue holds in-flight messages without encryption at rest.
rules.title: CloudFormation SQS queue must enable encryption

remediate.snippet: Set SqsManagedSseEnabled: true (or KmsMasterKeyId) on the AWS::SQS::Queue.

requiredActions: Set SqsManagedSseEnabled: true (or KmsMasterKeyId) on the AWS::SQS::Queue.

rules.threat: An unencrypted Redshift cluster exposes the warehouse at rest.
rules.title: CloudFormation Redshift cluster must be encrypted

remediate.snippet: Set Encrypted: true on the AWS::Redshift::Cluster.

requiredActions: Set Encrypted: true on the AWS::Redshift::Cluster.

rules.threat: Without enhanced VPC routing, COPY/UNLOAD traffic bypasses VPC egress controls.
rules.title: CloudFormation Redshift should use enhanced VPC routing

remediate.snippet: Set EnhancedVpcRouting: true on the AWS::Redshift::Cluster.

requiredActions: Set EnhancedVpcRouting: true on the AWS::Redshift::Cluster.

rules.threat: Disabling upgrades keeps the cluster on vulnerable engine versions.
rules.title: CloudFormation Redshift must allow version upgrades

remediate.snippet: Set AllowVersionUpgrade: true on the AWS::Redshift::Cluster.

requiredActions: Set AllowVersionUpgrade: true on the AWS::Redshift::Cluster.

rules.threat: An unencrypted DocumentDB cluster exposes documents at rest.
rules.title: CloudFormation DocumentDB cluster must be encrypted

remediate.snippet: Set StorageEncrypted: true on the AWS::DocDB::DBCluster.

requiredActions: Set StorageEncrypted: true on the AWS::DocDB::DBCluster.

rules.threat: Without deletion protection a compromised credential can destroy the cluster.
rules.title: CloudFormation DocumentDB cluster must enable deletion protection

remediate.snippet: Set DeletionProtection: true on the AWS::DocDB::DBCluster.

requiredActions: Set DeletionProtection: true on the AWS::DocDB::DBCluster.

rules.threat: Cached data — often session tokens — is exposed if storage is accessed.
rules.title: CloudFormation ElastiCache must encrypt at rest

remediate.snippet: Set AtRestEncryptionEnabled: true on the AWS::ElastiCache::ReplicationGroup.

requiredActions: Set AtRestEncryptionEnabled: true on the AWS::ElastiCache::ReplicationGroup.

rules.threat: Cache traffic is plaintext and sniffable without transit encryption.
rules.title: CloudFormation ElastiCache must encrypt in transit

remediate.snippet: Set TransitEncryptionEnabled: true on the AWS::ElastiCache::ReplicationGroup.

requiredActions: Set TransitEncryptionEnabled: true on the AWS::ElastiCache::ReplicationGroup.

rules.threat: An unencrypted Neptune cluster exposes graph data at rest.
rules.title: CloudFormation Neptune cluster must be encrypted

remediate.snippet: Set StorageEncrypted: true on the AWS::Neptune::DBCluster.

requiredActions: Set StorageEncrypted: true on the AWS::Neptune::DBCluster.

rules.threat: An OpenSearch domain without at-rest encryption exposes indexed data.
rules.title: CloudFormation OpenSearch domain must encrypt at rest

remediate.snippet: Add EncryptionAtRestOptions: { Enabled: true } to the domain.

requiredActions: Add EncryptionAtRestOptions: { Enabled: true } to the domain.

rules.threat: Intra-cluster traffic is plaintext without node-to-node encryption.
rules.title: CloudFormation OpenSearch domain must use node-to-node encryption

remediate.snippet: Add NodeToNodeEncryptionOptions: { Enabled: true } to the domain.

requiredActions: Add NodeToNodeEncryptionOptions: { Enabled: true } to the domain.

rules.threat: Plaintext queries carry credentials without enforced HTTPS.
rules.title: CloudFormation OpenSearch domain must enforce HTTPS

remediate.snippet: Set DomainEndpointOptions: { EnforceHTTPS: true } on the domain.

requiredActions: Set DomainEndpointOptions: { EnforceHTTPS: true } on the domain.

rules.threat: DMS replication instances default to publicly accessible.
rules.title: CloudFormation DMS instance must not be publicly accessible

remediate.snippet: Set PubliclyAccessible: false on the AWS::DMS::ReplicationInstance.

requiredActions: Set PubliclyAccessible: false on the AWS::DMS::ReplicationInstance.

rules.threat: An unencrypted Kinesis stream stores records in plaintext.
rules.title: CloudFormation Kinesis stream must encrypt at rest

remediate.snippet: Add StreamEncryption: { EncryptionType: KMS, KeyId: ... } to the AWS::Kinesis::Stream.

requiredActions: Add StreamEncryption: { EncryptionType: KMS, KeyId: ... } to the AWS::Kinesis::Stream.

rules.threat: Without deletion protection a compromised credential can destroy the database.
rules.title: CloudFormation RDS instance must enable deletion protection

remediate.snippet: Set DeletionProtection: true on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

requiredActions: Set DeletionProtection: true on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

rules.threat: Disabling minor upgrades leaves the DB on vulnerable patch levels.
rules.title: CloudFormation RDS must allow automatic minor upgrades

remediate.snippet: Set AutoMinorVersionUpgrade: true on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

requiredActions: Set AutoMinorVersionUpgrade: true on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

rules.threat: A zero backup window means no point-in-time recovery.
rules.title: CloudFormation RDS must set a backup retention period

remediate.snippet: Set BackupRetentionPeriod to at least 7 on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

requiredActions: Set BackupRetentionPeriod to at least 7 on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

rules.threat: An unencrypted Aurora cluster exposes the database at rest.
rules.title: CloudFormation Aurora cluster must be encrypted

remediate.snippet: Set StorageEncrypted: true on the AWS::RDS::DBCluster.

requiredActions: Set StorageEncrypted: true on the AWS::RDS::DBCluster.

rules.threat: Without deletion protection a compromised credential can destroy the cluster.
rules.title: CloudFormation Aurora cluster must enable deletion protection

remediate.snippet: Set DeletionProtection: true on the AWS::RDS::DBCluster.

requiredActions: Set DeletionProtection: true on the AWS::RDS::DBCluster.

rules.threat: A zero cluster backup window means no point-in-time recovery.
rules.title: CloudFormation Aurora cluster must set backup retention

remediate.snippet: Set BackupRetentionPeriod to at least 7 on the AWS::RDS::DBCluster.

requiredActions: Set BackupRetentionPeriod to at least 7 on the AWS::RDS::DBCluster.

rules.threat: A single-region trail misses global service events.
rules.title: CloudFormation CloudTrail must be multi-region

remediate.snippet: Set IsMultiRegionTrail: true on the AWS::CloudTrail::Trail.

requiredActions: Set IsMultiRegionTrail: true on the AWS::CloudTrail::Trail.

rules.threat: Without validation an attacker can tamper with trail logs undetected.
rules.title: CloudFormation CloudTrail must enable log file validation

remediate.snippet: Set EnableLogFileValidation: true on the AWS::CloudTrail::Trail.

requiredActions: Set EnableLogFileValidation: true on the AWS::CloudTrail::Trail.

rules.threat: An allow-all behavior serves content and cookies over plaintext.
rules.title: CloudFormation CloudFront must redirect viewers to HTTPS

remediate.snippet: Set ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https on every cache behavior.

requiredActions: Set ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https on every cache behavior.

rules.threat: Direct internet access is an exfiltration and C2 channel from the VPC.
rules.title: CloudFormation SageMaker notebook must disable direct internet

remediate.snippet: Set DirectInternetAccess: Disabled on the notebook instance.

requiredActions: Set DirectInternetAccess: Disabled on the notebook instance.

rules.threat: Root access lets a user tamper with the notebook instance.
rules.title: CloudFormation SageMaker notebook must disable root access

remediate.snippet: Set RootAccess: Disabled on the notebook instance.

requiredActions: Set RootAccess: Disabled on the notebook instance.

rules.threat: Without X-Ray tracing, abuse of a Lambda is harder to detect.
rules.title: CloudFormation Lambda should enable active tracing

remediate.snippet: Add TracingConfig: { Mode: Active } to the AWS::Lambda::Function.

requiredActions: Add TracingConfig: { Mode: Active } to the AWS::Lambda::Function.

rules.threat: A legacy Elasticsearch domain without at-rest encryption exposes indexed data.
rules.title: CloudFormation Elasticsearch domain must encrypt at rest

remediate.snippet: Add EncryptionAtRestOptions: { Enabled: true } to the domain.

requiredActions: Add EncryptionAtRestOptions: { Enabled: true } to the domain.

rules.threat: Plaintext queries carry credentials without enforced HTTPS.
rules.title: CloudFormation Elasticsearch domain must enforce HTTPS

remediate.snippet: Set DomainEndpointOptions: { EnforceHTTPS: true } on the domain.

requiredActions: Set DomainEndpointOptions: { EnforceHTTPS: true } on the domain.

rules.threat: Disabling deletion protection lets an attacker destroy the immutable ledger.
rules.title: CloudFormation QLDB ledger must keep deletion protection

remediate.snippet: Set DeletionProtection: true on the AWS::QLDB::Ledger.

requiredActions: Set DeletionProtection: true on the AWS::QLDB::Ledger.

rules.threat: Without a default root object, requests to the root can expose origin content.
rules.title: CloudFormation CloudFront should define a default root object

remediate.snippet: Set DefaultRootObject on the AWS::CloudFront::Distribution.

requiredActions: Set DefaultRootObject on the AWS::CloudFront::Distribution.

rules.threat: No access logging leaves abuse and scraping unrecorded.
rules.title: CloudFormation CloudFront should enable access logging

remediate.snippet: Add a Logging block (dedicated bucket) to the AWS::CloudFront::Distribution.

requiredActions: Add a Logging block (dedicated bucket) to the AWS::CloudFront::Distribution.

rules.threat: No WAF means no managed protection against L7 attacks.
rules.title: CloudFormation CloudFront should attach a WAF

remediate.snippet: Set WebACLId to a WAFv2 web ACL (CLOUDFRONT scope).

requiredActions: Set WebACLId to a WAFv2 web ACL (CLOUDFRONT scope).

rules.threat: Allowing legacy TLS exposes viewers to downgrade attacks.
rules.title: CloudFormation CloudFront must require TLS 1.2

remediate.snippet: Set ViewerCertificate.MinimumProtocolVersion to a TLSv1.2_* policy.

requiredActions: Set ViewerCertificate.MinimumProtocolVersion to a TLSv1.2_* policy.

rules.threat: Infinite retention accumulates sensitive data forever.
rules.title: CloudFormation log group must set a retention period

remediate.snippet: Set RetentionInDays on the AWS::Logs::LogGroup.

requiredActions: Set RetentionInDays on the AWS::Logs::LogGroup.

rules.threat: Log data is stored unencrypted with broad default access.
rules.title: CloudFormation log group should be CMK-encrypted

remediate.snippet: Set KmsKeyId (CMK) on the AWS::Logs::LogGroup.

requiredActions: Set KmsKeyId (CMK) on the AWS::Logs::LogGroup.

rules.threat: Default-key encryption is readable by broad KMS access.
rules.title: CloudFormation secret should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Set KmsKeyId (CMK) on the AWS::SecretsManager::Secret.

requiredActions: Set KmsKeyId (CMK) on the AWS::SecretsManager::Secret.

rules.threat: Default keys may not be customer-managed.
rules.title: CloudFormation ECR repository should declare KMS encryption

remediate.snippet: Add EncryptionConfiguration: { EncryptionType: KMS } to the repository.

requiredActions: Add EncryptionConfiguration: { EncryptionType: KMS } to the repository.

rules.threat: Plaintext broker traffic exposes Kafka messages.
rules.title: CloudFormation MSK cluster must encrypt in transit

remediate.snippet: Set EncryptionInfo.EncryptionInTransit.ClientBroker: TLS.

requiredActions: Set EncryptionInfo.EncryptionInTransit.ClientBroker: TLS.

rules.threat: Clients can override encryption/output settings otherwise.
rules.title: CloudFormation Athena workgroup must enforce its config

remediate.snippet: Set WorkGroupConfiguration.EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration: true.

requiredActions: Set WorkGroupConfiguration.EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration: true.

rules.threat: Abuse against the stage is harder to detect without tracing.
rules.title: CloudFormation API Gateway stage should enable X-Ray

remediate.snippet: Set TracingEnabled: true on the AWS::ApiGateway::Stage.

requiredActions: Set TracingEnabled: true on the AWS::ApiGateway::Stage.

rules.threat: API abuse goes unrecorded without access logging.
rules.title: CloudFormation API Gateway stage should log access

remediate.snippet: Add AccessLogSetting (destination + format) to the stage.

requiredActions: Add AccessLogSetting (destination + format) to the stage.

rules.threat: Tampered packages run unchecked without code signing.
rules.title: CloudFormation Lambda should enforce code signing

remediate.snippet: Set CodeSigningConfigArn on the AWS::Lambda::Function.

requiredActions: Set CodeSigningConfigArn on the AWS::Lambda::Function.

rules.threat: Kubernetes Secrets are only base64 in etcd otherwise.
rules.title: CloudFormation EKS cluster should encrypt secrets

remediate.snippet: Add EncryptionConfig (KMS, resources=[secrets]) to the cluster.

requiredActions: Add EncryptionConfig (KMS, resources=[secrets]) to the cluster.

rules.threat: API audit events are not recorded otherwise.
rules.title: CloudFormation EKS cluster should enable control-plane logging

remediate.snippet: Enable Logging.ClusterLogging (api, audit) on the cluster.

requiredActions: Enable Logging.ClusterLogging (api, audit) on the cluster.

rules.threat: No point-in-time recovery without a backup window.
rules.title: CloudFormation DocumentDB must set backup retention

remediate.snippet: Set BackupRetentionPeriod >= 7 on the AWS::DocDB::DBCluster.

requiredActions: Set BackupRetentionPeriod >= 7 on the AWS::DocDB::DBCluster.

rules.threat: No point-in-time recovery without a backup window.
rules.title: CloudFormation Neptune must set backup retention

remediate.snippet: Set BackupRetentionPeriod >= 7 on the AWS::Neptune::DBCluster.

requiredActions: Set BackupRetentionPeriod >= 7 on the AWS::Neptune::DBCluster.

rules.threat: Access-control tags are lost on snapshots otherwise.
rules.title: CloudFormation RDS should copy tags to snapshots

remediate.snippet: Set CopyTagsToSnapshot: true on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

requiredActions: Set CopyTagsToSnapshot: true on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

rules.threat: Bulk object reads leave no forensic trail without logging.
rules.title: CloudFormation S3 bucket should enable access logging

remediate.snippet: Add LoggingConfiguration (dedicated log bucket) to the AWS::S3::Bucket.

requiredActions: Add LoggingConfiguration (dedicated log bucket) to the AWS::S3::Bucket.

rules.threat: A public broker is reachable from the internet.
rules.title: CloudFormation Amazon MQ broker must not be public

remediate.snippet: Set PubliclyAccessible: false on the AWS::AmazonMQ::Broker.

requiredActions: Set PubliclyAccessible: false on the AWS::AmazonMQ::Broker.

rules.threat: Cache traffic is plaintext without TLS.
rules.title: CloudFormation MemoryDB cluster must enable TLS

remediate.snippet: Set TLSEnabled: true on the AWS::MemoryDB::Cluster.

requiredActions: Set TLSEnabled: true on the AWS::MemoryDB::Cluster.

rules.threat: Disabling upgrades leaves the engine on vulnerable versions.
rules.title: CloudFormation ElastiCache must allow minor upgrades

remediate.snippet: Set AutoMinorVersionUpgrade: true on the replication group.

requiredActions: Set AutoMinorVersionUpgrade: true on the replication group.

rules.threat: No restore point without snapshot retention.
rules.title: CloudFormation ElastiCache (Redis) should retain snapshots

remediate.snippet: Set SnapshotRetentionLimit >= 1 on the replication group.

requiredActions: Set SnapshotRetentionLimit >= 1 on the replication group.

rules.threat: OS-level metrics for attack detection are missing otherwise.
rules.title: CloudFormation RDS should enable enhanced monitoring

remediate.snippet: Set MonitoringInterval (and MonitoringRoleArn) on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

requiredActions: Set MonitoringInterval (and MonitoringRoleArn) on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation EC2 instance that does not require IMDSv2 exposes its instance-profile credentials to an SSRF reaching 169.254.169.254.
rules.title: CloudFormation EC2 instance must enforce IMDSv2

remediate.snippet: Add LaunchTemplateData/MetadataOptions { HttpTokens: required, HttpPutResponseHopLimit: 1 } (or set MetadataOptions on the instance) to enforce IMDSv2.

requiredActions: Set MetadataOptions.HttpTokens: required on the AWS::EC2::Instance.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation DynamoDB table without explicit SSE relies on default keys; a customer-managed key gives a tighter control boundary over table data.
rules.title: CloudFormation DynamoDB table should declare server-side encryption

remediate.snippet: Add SSESpecification { SSEEnabled: true, SSEType: KMS, KMSMasterKeyId: <cmk> } to the AWS::DynamoDB::Table.

requiredActions: Add SSESpecification with SSEEnabled: true (KMS) to the AWS::DynamoDB::Table.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation backup vault without a KMS key encrypts recovery points with default keys, weakening the protection of the very backups meant to survive a breach.
rules.title: CloudFormation backup vault must use a KMS key

remediate.snippet: Set EncryptionKeyArn to a customer-managed KMS key ARN on the AWS::Backup::BackupVault.

requiredActions: Set EncryptionKeyArn to a CMK ARN on the AWS::Backup::BackupVault.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation SageMaker endpoint config without a KMS key leaves the inference instance storage — model artifacts and buffered request data — under default-key encryption.
rules.title: CloudFormation SageMaker endpoint config should use a KMS key

remediate.snippet: Set KmsKeyId to a customer-managed KMS key on the AWS::SageMaker::EndpointConfig.

requiredActions: Set KmsKeyId to a CMK on the AWS::SageMaker::EndpointConfig.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation RDS instance without IAM database authentication relies on static passwords that, once leaked, grant standing access with no central revocation.
rules.title: CloudFormation RDS instance should enable IAM database authentication

remediate.snippet: Set EnableIAMDatabaseAuthentication: true on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

requiredActions: Set EnableIAMDatabaseAuthentication: true on the AWS::RDS::DBInstance.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation HTTP API (v2) stage without access logging leaves API abuse and injection attempts with no forensic trail.
rules.title: CloudFormation API Gateway v2 stage should enable access logging

remediate.snippet: Add AccessLogSettings { DestinationArn, Format } to the AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Stage.

requiredActions: Enable AccessLogSettings on the AWS::ApiGatewayV2::Stage.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation EFS file system policy with Principal AWS "*" lets any account reaching the mount targets read or write the shared file system.
rules.title: CloudFormation EFS policy must not allow any principal

remediate.snippet: Replace Principal AWS "*" with specific account/role ARNs on the AWS::EFS::FileSystemPolicy.

requiredActions: Scope the AWS::EFS::FileSystemPolicy to explicit principals; never a bare AWS "*".

rules.threat: A CloudFormation EKS cluster with a public API endpoint exposes the Kubernetes control plane to internet-wide attack.
rules.title: CloudFormation EKS cluster must not expose a public endpoint

remediate.snippet: Set ResourcesVpcConfig.EndpointPublicAccess: false (with EndpointPrivateAccess: true) or scope PublicAccessCidrs on the AWS::EKS::Cluster.

requiredActions: Disable EndpointPublicAccess (or scope PublicAccessCidrs) on the AWS::EKS::Cluster.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation RDS Proxy without RequireTLS accepts plaintext client connections, exposing credentials and queries on the wire.
rules.title: CloudFormation RDS Proxy must require TLS

remediate.snippet: Set RequireTLS: true on the AWS::RDS::DBProxy.

requiredActions: Set RequireTLS: true on the AWS::RDS::DBProxy.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation API Gateway stage cache holding unencrypted responses can leak cached PII or tokens if the cache is accessed.
rules.title: CloudFormation API Gateway stage cache must be encrypted

remediate.snippet: Set CacheDataEncrypted: true in the MethodSettings of the AWS::ApiGateway::Stage when caching is enabled.

requiredActions: Set CacheDataEncrypted: true in the AWS::ApiGateway::Stage MethodSettings.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation Neptune cluster without audit log exports leaves no record of graph access, blinding incident response.
rules.title: CloudFormation Neptune cluster should export audit logs

remediate.snippet: Set EnableCloudwatchLogsExports: [audit] on the AWS::Neptune::DBCluster.

requiredActions: Set EnableCloudwatchLogsExports to include audit on the AWS::Neptune::DBCluster.

rules.threat: A CloudFormation DocumentDB cluster without audit log exports leaves no record of database access, blinding incident response.
rules.title: CloudFormation DocumentDB cluster should export audit logs

remediate.snippet: Set EnableCloudwatchLogsExports: [audit, profiler] on the AWS::DocDB::DBCluster.

requiredActions: Set EnableCloudwatchLogsExports to include audit on the AWS::DocDB::DBCluster.

rules.threat: Without WAFv2 logging an attacker probing for SQLi/XSS/bot patterns leaves no trace, so blocked-vs-allowed decisions can't be audited and an evasion campaign goes undetected until exploitation succeeds.
rules.title: Enable WAFv2 web ACL logging
requiredActions: Attach an aws_wafv2_web_acl_logging_configuration to every WAFv2 web ACL and route logs to a retained CloudWatch log group, S3 bucket, or Firehose.

rules.threat: A web ACL whose default action is Allow lets any request not matching an explicit block rule through, so an attacker simply crafts payloads the rule set never anticipated and bypasses the WAF entirely.
rules.title: Review WAFv2 default action and rule coverage

remediate.snippet: Define an explicit default_action and ensure managed rule groups (AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet, KnownBadInputs, SQLi) are associated so unmatched traffic is handled deliberately.

requiredActions: Define an explicit default_action block and attach AWS managed rule groups covering common exploits and bad inputs.

rules.threat: Without a backend client certificate, the API Gateway integration cannot prove its identity to the origin, letting an attacker who reaches the origin network impersonate the gateway and inject requests that appear to come from the trusted front door.
rules.title: Attach a client certificate to API Gateway stages
requiredActions: Generate an aws_api_gateway_client_certificate and reference its id from the stage so the backend can verify gateway-originated calls.

rules.threat: A REST API stage with no WAF web ACL association is directly exposed to injection, scraping, and credential-stuffing traffic with no L7 filtering, giving an attacker an unguarded entry point to backend Lambdas and integrations.
rules.title: Associate a WAFv2 web ACL with API Gateway stages
requiredActions: Create an aws_wafv2_web_acl_association binding the stage ARN to a WAFv2 web ACL with managed protection rules.

rules.threat: An unencrypted API Gateway method cache stores response bodies in plaintext, so an attacker with access to the cache layer can read cached responses containing tokens, PII, or internal data without ever hitting the backend.
rules.title: Encrypt API Gateway method cache data

remediate.snippet: In settings { cache_data_encrypted = true } and enable caching_enabled only with encryption on.

requiredActions: Set cache_data_encrypted = true in the method settings block whenever caching is enabled.

rules.threat: An HTTP API route with NONE authorization is fully anonymous, letting any internet client invoke the backend integration directly and abuse business logic or exhaust downstream resources.
rules.title: Require authorization on HTTP API routes

remediate.snippet: Set authorization_type to JWT or AWS_IAM and attach an authorizer; reserve NONE only for intentionally public health-check routes.

requiredActions: Set authorization_type to JWT or AWS_IAM on every non-public HTTP API route and attach an authorizer.

rules.threat: A Cognito user pool with MFA off lets an attacker who phishes or credential-stuffs a single password fully take over the account, since no second factor stands between stolen credentials and access.
rules.title: Enforce MFA on Cognito user pools
requiredActions: Set mfa_configuration = "ON" and configure software_token_mfa_configuration so all users present a second factor.

rules.threat: Without an explicit password policy, Cognito accepts weak short passwords, so an attacker's offline or online guessing succeeds far faster against the directory of user credentials.
rules.title: Define a strong Cognito password policy

remediate.snippet: Add a password_policy block with minimum_length >= 14, require_uppercase, require_lowercase, require_numbers, and require_symbols set to true.

requiredActions: Add a password_policy block enforcing at least 14 characters plus upper, lower, number, and symbol requirements.

rules.threat: With advanced security mode off, Cognito cannot detect compromised credentials or impossible-travel sign-ins, so an attacker using leaked passwords logs in unchallenged with no adaptive risk response.
rules.title: Enable Cognito advanced security (threat protection)

remediate.snippet: Set user_pool_add_ons { advanced_security_mode = "ENFORCED" } to enable adaptive authentication and compromised-credential detection.

requiredActions: Set advanced_security_mode to ENFORCED in the user_pool_add_ons block.

rules.threat: A container with a writable root filesystem lets an attacker who achieves code execution drop tools, persist payloads, or tamper with binaries inside the running task, escalating a single RCE into durable foothold.
rules.title: Make ECS container root filesystem read-only

remediate.snippet: In each container definition set readonlyRootFilesystem: true and mount tmpfs/volumes for the few paths needing writes.

requiredActions: Set readonlyRootFilesystem: true in every container definition and provide explicit writable mounts where required.

rules.threat: Secrets passed as plaintext environment variables sit in the task definition, ECS console, and CloudWatch, so any operator or attacker with describe-task-definition can harvest credentials for lateral movement.
rules.title: Use Secrets Manager/SSM for ECS container secrets

remediate.snippet: Replace plaintext environment entries with the secrets array referencing Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store ARNs (valueFrom).

requiredActions: Move sensitive values out of environment and into the secrets array using valueFrom Secrets Manager/SSM ARNs.

rules.threat: Without Container Insights an ECS cluster emits no aggregated performance/anomaly telemetry, so a cryptomining or runaway workload injected by an attacker blends into the noise and goes unnoticed.
rules.title: Enable Container Insights on ECS clusters

remediate.snippet: Add setting { name = "containerInsights" value = "enabled" } to the cluster.

requiredActions: Add a setting block enabling containerInsights on the ECS cluster.

rules.threat: An EKS public endpoint open to 0.0.0.0/0 exposes the Kubernetes API server to the entire internet, letting an attacker hammer authentication and exploit any control-plane CVE from anywhere.
rules.title: Restrict EKS public endpoint CIDRs

remediate.snippet: Set public_access_cidrs in vpc_config to your corporate/VPN egress ranges, or disable the public endpoint and use private access.

requiredActions: Replace 0.0.0.0/0 in public_access_cidrs with a tight allowlist, or disable the public endpoint entirely.
