rules.threat: A Lambda with no dead-letter queue silently drops failed async invocations, so an attacker triggering errors can erase evidence of malicious events and defenders lose the forensic trail of what was processed.
rules.title: Configure a dead-letter queue for Lambda

remediate.snippet: Add a dead_letter_config block with target_arn pointing at an SQS queue or SNS topic to capture failed async events.

requiredActions: Add a dead_letter_config block referencing an SQS or SNS target for failed asynchronous invocations.

rules.threat: A Lambda not attached to a VPC reaches private resources only over the public internet and is harder to constrain with network controls, widening the blast radius if its code or dependencies are compromised.
rules.title: Run sensitive Lambdas inside a VPC

remediate.snippet: Add a vpc_config block with subnet_ids and security_group_ids so the function runs in private subnets with controlled egress.

requiredActions: Add a vpc_config block placing the function in private subnets with restrictive security groups.

rules.threat: A Lambda without reserved concurrency can be driven to consume the whole account concurrency pool, letting an attacker who floods one function starve every other function in the account — a denial-of-wallet and availability attack.
rules.title: Set reserved concurrency limits on Lambda
requiredActions: Set reserved_concurrent_executions to a sane per-function ceiling to contain runaway or attacker-driven invocation floods.

rules.threat: An SQS queue policy that doesn't deny non-TLS requests permits messages to be sent and received over cleartext, letting a network-positioned attacker read or tamper with queued data in transit.
rules.title: Enforce TLS-only access on SQS queue policies

remediate.snippet: Add a Deny statement with condition Bool aws:SecureTransport false to reject all non-HTTPS requests.

requiredActions: Add a Deny-on-aws:SecureTransport-false statement to the queue policy to force TLS.

rules.threat: An SNS topic policy without a non-TLS deny allows publish/subscribe over cleartext, exposing notification payloads to interception or modification by an on-path attacker.
rules.title: Enforce TLS-only access on SNS topic policies

remediate.snippet: Add a Deny statement with condition Bool aws:SecureTransport false to the topic policy.

requiredActions: Add a Deny-on-aws:SecureTransport-false statement to the SNS topic policy.

rules.threat: An S3 bucket policy that doesn't deny non-TLS requests lets objects be uploaded and fetched over HTTP, so a network attacker can sniff or alter object data and credentials in transit.
rules.title: Enforce TLS-only access via S3 bucket policy

remediate.snippet: Add a Deny statement on s3:* with condition Bool aws:SecureTransport false covering both the bucket and /* object ARNs.

requiredActions: Add a Deny-on-aws:SecureTransport-false statement to the bucket policy covering bucket and object ARNs.

rules.threat: A bucket without Object Lock lets an attacker (or compromised credential) overwrite or delete objects, defeating ransomware recovery and tamper-evidence for logs and backups.
rules.title: Enable S3 Object Lock for immutable buckets
requiredActions: Set object_lock_enabled = true at bucket creation and configure a retention rule via aws_s3_bucket_object_lock_configuration for WORM buckets.

rules.threat: A DynamoDB table without deletion protection can be destroyed by a single compromised credential or stray automation, wiping production data with no built-in guardrail.
rules.title: Enable DynamoDB deletion protection
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection_enabled = true on production DynamoDB tables.

rules.threat: A DynamoDB table left on AWS-owned default encryption gives no customer-controlled key, so revocation and audit of data access are impossible and a key compromise can't be independently contained.
rules.title: Use a customer-managed KMS key for DynamoDB SSE

remediate.snippet: Add server_side_encryption { enabled = true kms_key_arn = <cmk> } to control and audit the encryption key.

requiredActions: Add a server_side_encryption block with a customer-managed kms_key_arn.

rules.threat: Without CloudTrail Insights, anomalous bursts of write or error API activity — the hallmark of an attacker enumerating or destroying resources — are not surfaced, delaying detection of an active compromise.
rules.title: Enable CloudTrail Insights

remediate.snippet: Add insight_selector { insight_type = "ApiCallRateInsight" } and ApiErrorRateInsight to flag anomalous API activity.

requiredActions: Add insight_selector blocks for ApiCallRateInsight and ApiErrorRateInsight.

rules.threat: A CloudFront custom origin configured to negotiate TLS 1.0/1.1 (or SSLv3) to the backend lets a network attacker downgrade and break the origin connection's confidentiality, exposing data between edge and origin.
rules.title: Require TLS 1.2+ on CloudFront origin connections

remediate.snippet: Set custom_origin_config.origin_ssl_protocols to ["TLSv1.2"] only and origin_protocol_policy = "https-only".

requiredActions: Restrict origin_ssl_protocols to TLSv1.2 and set origin_protocol_policy to https-only.

rules.threat: A CloudFront distribution fronting an S3 origin without an origin access control/identity lets clients bypass CloudFront and hit the bucket directly, defeating WAF, signed URLs, and geo restrictions.
rules.title: Use origin access control for CloudFront S3 origins

remediate.snippet: Reference an aws_cloudfront_origin_access_control via origin_access_control_id and lock the bucket policy to that OAC principal.

requiredActions: Attach an origin access control (or legacy OAI) to S3 origins and restrict the bucket policy to it.

rules.threat: An HTTP listener whose default action forwards instead of redirecting to HTTPS serves application traffic in cleartext, letting an on-path attacker read sessions and inject content before any TLS upgrade.
rules.title: Redirect ALB HTTP listeners to HTTPS

remediate.snippet: Change the port-80 listener default_action to type = "redirect" with redirect { protocol = "HTTPS" port = "443" status_code = "HTTP_301" }.

requiredActions: Convert port-80 listener default actions to an HTTPS redirect (HTTP_301).

rules.threat: A public hosted zone without query logging gives defenders no visibility into DNS resolution, so data-exfiltration-over-DNS or malware C2 lookups occur invisibly within the account's domains.
rules.title: Enable Route53 public DNS query logging
requiredActions: Create an aws_route53_query_log for each public hosted zone routing to a CloudWatch log group.

rules.threat: Glue jobs without a security configuration write CloudWatch logs, job bookmarks, and S3 outputs unencrypted, exposing intermediate datasets and potentially sensitive ETL results to anyone with storage access.
rules.title: Attach an encrypted Glue security configuration

remediate.snippet: Define encryption_configuration with cloudwatch_encryption, job_bookmarks_encryption, and s3_encryption all set to KMS modes, and attach it to jobs/crawlers.

requiredActions: Define an encryption_configuration covering CloudWatch, job bookmarks, and S3 with KMS, and reference it from Glue jobs and crawlers.

rules.threat: An Athena workgroup without result encryption stores query output in S3 in plaintext, so an attacker reading the results bucket harvests query results that may contain regulated data the user never intended to persist openly.
rules.title: Encrypt Athena workgroup query results

remediate.snippet: Add result_configuration { encryption_configuration { encryption_option = "SSE_KMS" kms_key_arn = <cmk> } } and enforce_workgroup_configuration = true.

requiredActions: Add result_configuration.encryption_configuration using SSE_KMS and enforce the workgroup configuration.

rules.threat: An EMR cluster launched without a security configuration runs with no at-rest/in-transit encryption or Kerberos, so an attacker on the cluster network can read HDFS blocks and intercept inter-node traffic.
rules.title: Attach a security configuration to EMR clusters
requiredActions: Create an aws_emr_security_configuration with at-rest, in-transit encryption and Kerberos, and set security_configuration on the cluster.

rules.threat: An MSK cluster with no client authentication accepts connections from any client reaching the brokers, letting an attacker on the VPC consume or produce to topics carrying business event data.
rules.title: Require client authentication on MSK clusters

remediate.snippet: Add a client_authentication block enabling tls (ACM CAs) or sasl { iam = true } so only authenticated clients connect.

requiredActions: Add a client_authentication block enabling mutual TLS or SASL/IAM.

rules.threat: An MSK cluster without broker log delivery produces no record of broker activity, so an attacker abusing topics or brokers leaves no audit trail for incident response.
rules.title: Enable MSK broker logging

remediate.snippet: Add logging_info { broker_logs { cloudwatch_logs { enabled = true log_group = ... } } } to ship broker logs.

requiredActions: Add a logging_info.broker_logs block delivering logs to CloudWatch, S3, or Firehose.

rules.threat: A Firehose delivery stream without server-side encryption buffers records in plaintext, exposing in-flight data (often logs or events containing identifiers) to anyone able to inspect the stream.
rules.title: Enable server-side encryption on Firehose streams

remediate.snippet: Add server_side_encryption { enabled = true key_type = "CUSTOMER_MANAGED_CMK" key_arn = <cmk> }.

requiredActions: Add a server_side_encryption block to the Firehose delivery stream, preferably with a customer-managed CMK.

rules.threat: A Step Functions state machine without logging records no execution history detail, so an attacker abusing a workflow to invoke privileged tasks leaves defenders blind to which states ran and with what input.
rules.title: Enable logging on Step Functions state machines

remediate.snippet: Add logging_configuration { level = "ALL" include_execution_data = true log_destination = ... } to a retained CloudWatch log group.

requiredActions: Add a logging_configuration block with level ALL to a CloudWatch log group.

rules.threat: An AWS Transfer Family server pinned to a legacy security policy negotiates weak SFTP/FTPS ciphers, letting a network attacker downgrade or break file-transfer confidentiality for credentials and data in transit.
rules.title: Use a modern Transfer Family security policy

remediate.snippet: Set security_policy_name to a recent policy (e.g. TransferSecurityPolicy-2024-01) that removes weak ciphers and key-exchange algorithms.

requiredActions: Set security_policy_name to a current Transfer Family security policy version.

rules.threat: A Transfer Family server with a PUBLIC endpoint type exposes the SFTP/FTPS service to the entire internet, giving attackers a global surface for credential brute force and protocol exploits.
rules.title: Use VPC endpoints for Transfer Family servers

remediate.snippet: Set endpoint_type = "VPC" with an endpoint_details block and restrict access via security groups / VPC endpoint allowlists.

requiredActions: Set endpoint_type to VPC and constrain access via security groups instead of a public endpoint.

rules.threat: An FSx for Windows file system without a KMS key uses no customer-controlled encryption, so a storage-layer compromise or key mishandling exposes hosted file shares with no independent revocation path.
rules.title: Encrypt FSx Windows file systems with KMS
requiredActions: Set kms_key_id to a customer-managed key on the FSx Windows file system.

rules.threat: An FSx for Lustre file system without a KMS key relies on opaque default encryption, leaving high-throughput scratch/persistent data without auditable, revocable key control.
rules.title: Encrypt FSx Lustre file systems with KMS
requiredActions: Set kms_key_id to a customer-managed key on the FSx Lustre file system.

rules.threat: A Backup vault without a lock configuration lets a compromised admin shorten retention or delete recovery points, so ransomware can destroy the very backups meant to recover from it.
rules.title: Apply a vault lock to AWS Backup vaults
requiredActions: Create an aws_backup_vault_lock_configuration enforcing minimum retention in compliance mode.

rules.threat: A KMS key set to bypass policy lockout (no rotation, single principal) combined with a short deletion window can be scheduled for deletion by one compromised principal, irreversibly destroying access to all data it protects.
rules.title: Set an adequate KMS key deletion window
requiredActions: Set deletion_window_in_days to at least 7 (prefer 30) to allow recovery before irreversible key destruction.

rules.threat: A Redshift parameter group with require_ssl off lets clients connect to the cluster over cleartext, exposing query results and credentials to network interception.
rules.title: Require SSL in Redshift parameter groups

remediate.snippet: Set the require_ssl parameter value to "true" in the parameter group.

requiredActions: Set the require_ssl parameter to true and attach the parameter group to all clusters.

rules.threat: Keeping the default 'awsuser' master username gives attackers half the credential pair for free, narrowing brute-force to just the password for the highest-privileged database account.
rules.title: Avoid the default Redshift master username

remediate.snippet: Set master_username to a non-default value and manage the password via Secrets Manager.

requiredActions: Change master_username away from the default awsuser value.

rules.threat: An RDS instance pinned to the expiring rds-ca-2019 bundle forces clients onto an old CA, and once it expires connections either fail or get downgraded to no-verify, opening a window for impersonation of the database endpoint.
rules.title: Use a current RDS CA certificate bundle
requiredActions: Set ca_cert_identifier to a current bundle such as rds-ca-rsa2048-g1.

rules.threat: An RDS parameter group leaving rds.force_ssl off (Postgres) or require_secure_transport off (MySQL) permits cleartext database sessions, so an attacker on the subnet can sniff queries and credentials.
rules.title: Force SSL in RDS parameter groups

remediate.snippet: Set rds.force_ssl (Postgres) or require_secure_transport (MySQL) parameter value to "1" and attach the group to instances.

requiredActions: Set the force-SSL parameter to 1 in the DB parameter group and attach it to all instances.

rules.threat: A Neptune cluster parameter group with neptune_enforce_ssl disabled accepts unencrypted Gremlin/SPARQL connections, exposing graph queries and data to interception on the VPC.
rules.title: Enforce SSL in Neptune cluster parameter groups

remediate.snippet: Set the neptune_enforce_ssl parameter value to "1" and attach the parameter group to the cluster.

requiredActions: Set neptune_enforce_ssl to 1 in the Neptune cluster parameter group.

rules.threat: A DocumentDB cluster parameter group with tls disabled permits plaintext MongoDB-protocol connections, letting an attacker on the network read documents and credentials in transit.
rules.title: Enable TLS in DocumentDB cluster parameter groups

remediate.snippet: Set the tls parameter value to "enabled" and attach the parameter group to the cluster.

requiredActions: Set the tls parameter to enabled in the DocumentDB cluster parameter group.

rules.threat: Without IAM Access Analyzer, externally-shared resources and risky cross-account trust relationships go unflagged, so an attacker who gains a foothold finds unmonitored paths to escalate across accounts.
rules.title: Enable IAM Access Analyzer

remediate.snippet: Provision an aws_accessanalyzer_analyzer (type ACCOUNT or ORGANIZATION) to continuously detect external and unused access.

requiredActions: Provision an IAM Access Analyzer at account or organization scope.

rules.threat: Without Security Hub aggregating findings, misconfigurations and GuardDuty/Inspector alerts scatter across services with no central triage, so an attacker's activity surfaces too slowly across the account fleet.
rules.title: Enable AWS Security Hub

remediate.snippet: Enable aws_securityhub_account with default standards and subscribe the FSBP and CIS standards across all regions.

requiredActions: Enable Security Hub and subscribe the FSBP and CIS standards in every active region.

rules.threat: An AWS Config recorder that doesn't record all resource types leaves blind spots in the configuration timeline, so an attacker can modify untracked resources without leaving a config-history audit trail.
rules.title: Record all resources in AWS Config

remediate.snippet: In recording_group set all_supported = true and include_global_resource_types = true.

requiredActions: Set all_supported = true (and include_global_resource_types = true) in the Config recording group.

rules.threat: Without Macie, sensitive data (PII, credentials, secrets) sitting in S3 goes unclassified, so a publicly-exposed or over-shared bucket leaks regulated data with no automated discovery to catch it.
rules.title: Enable Amazon Macie
requiredActions: Set the Macie account status to ENABLED and configure sensitive-data discovery jobs.

rules.threat: Without Inspector v2 enabled for EC2/ECR/Lambda, known-vulnerable packages and images deploy unscanned, leaving an attacker a catalogue of exploitable CVEs running in production.
rules.title: Enable Amazon Inspector v2

remediate.snippet: Provision aws_inspector2_enabler with resource_types covering EC2, ECR, and LAMBDA for continuous vulnerability scanning.

requiredActions: Enable Inspector v2 for EC2, ECR, and Lambda resource types.

rules.threat: A default security group left with ingress/egress rules silently attaches permissive access to any resource that forgets to specify a group, giving an attacker an unexpected lateral path.
rules.title: Strip all rules from the default security group

remediate.snippet: Declare aws_default_security_group with no ingress and no egress blocks so the default group denies all traffic.

requiredActions: Remove all ingress and egress rules from the default security group resource.

rules.threat: A Network Firewall without logging configuration drops or allows flows with no record, so an attacker probing or exfiltrating through the firewall leaves no alert/flow log for detection.
rules.title: Enable Network Firewall logging

remediate.snippet: Provision aws_networkfirewall_logging_configuration with FLOW and ALERT log_destination_config entries to S3/CloudWatch.

requiredActions: Configure Network Firewall logging for both FLOW and ALERT log types.

rules.threat: An unencrypted EBS snapshot can be copied or shared and restored elsewhere with its data fully readable, so an attacker who can read snapshots exfiltrates volume contents without touching the live instance.
rules.title: Encrypt EBS snapshots
requiredActions: Set encrypted = true (with a kms_key_id) on EBS snapshot copies.

rules.threat: A Beanstalk environment without managed platform updates runs aging platform versions, so an attacker exploits known platform CVEs that auto-patching would have closed.
rules.title: Enable Beanstalk managed platform updates

remediate.snippet: Add a setting for namespace aws:elasticbeanstalk:managedactions name ManagedActionsEnabled value true.

requiredActions: Enable ManagedActionsEnabled in the aws:elasticbeanstalk:managedactions namespace.

rules.threat: An AppFlow flow without a customer KMS key encrypts transferred records with AWS-managed keys only, removing the ability to audit and revoke access to data moving between SaaS and AWS.
rules.title: Use a customer KMS key for AppFlow flows
requiredActions: Set kms_arn to a customer-managed key on the AppFlow flow.

rules.threat: A Timestream database without a customer KMS key relies on default encryption, so time-series telemetry (often device/location data) lacks auditable, revocable key control.
rules.title: Encrypt Timestream databases with customer KMS
requiredActions: Set kms_key_id to a customer-managed key on the Timestream database.

rules.threat: A CodePipeline using the default artifact store key gives no customer-controlled encryption of build artifacts, so artifacts that may contain secrets or proprietary code lack an auditable, revocable key boundary.
rules.title: Use a customer KMS key for CodePipeline artifacts

remediate.snippet: Add encryption_key { id = <cmk> type = "KMS" } inside the artifact_store block.

requiredActions: Add an encryption_key block referencing a customer-managed KMS key to the artifact_store.

rules.threat: A CodeArtifact domain without a customer KMS key stores package assets under default encryption, removing independent control and audit over access to private build dependencies.
rules.title: Encrypt CodeArtifact domains with customer KMS
requiredActions: Set encryption_key to a customer-managed KMS key on the CodeArtifact domain.

rules.threat: An EFS file system policy without an aws:SecureTransport deny permits NFS mounts without TLS, letting a network attacker read file traffic between clients and the file system.
rules.title: Enforce in-transit encryption via EFS policy

remediate.snippet: Add a Deny statement on elasticfilesystem:* with condition Bool aws:SecureTransport false to require TLS mounts.

requiredActions: Add a Deny-on-aws:SecureTransport-false statement to the EFS file system policy.

rules.threat: A SageMaker notebook instance without a KMS key stores notebook content and attached data on an unencrypted-by-customer-key volume, so training data and embedded credentials lack revocable key control.
rules.title: Encrypt SageMaker notebook storage with KMS
requiredActions: Set kms_key_id to a customer-managed key on the SageMaker notebook instance.

rules.threat: A GuardDuty detector without S3 data-event monitoring misses anomalous bucket access patterns, so an attacker quietly enumerating or exfiltrating S3 objects evades the threat-detection layer.
rules.title: Enable GuardDuty S3 protection

remediate.snippet: Set the S3_DATA_EVENTS detector feature status to ENABLED.

requiredActions: Enable the S3_DATA_EVENTS GuardDuty detector feature.

rules.threat: Without GuardDuty EBS malware protection, malware landing on EC2 volumes during an intrusion is not detected, letting an attacker stage tools and persist undetected on disk.
rules.title: Enable GuardDuty EBS malware protection

remediate.snippet: Set the EBS_MALWARE_PROTECTION detector feature status to ENABLED.

requiredActions: Enable the EBS_MALWARE_PROTECTION GuardDuty detector feature.

rules.threat: A hosted zone without DNSSEC signing lets an attacker who can spoof or poison resolvers forge responses for the domain, redirecting users to attacker-controlled hosts undetected.
rules.title: Enable DNSSEC signing on Route53 zones
requiredActions: Create a key-signing key and enable aws_route53_hosted_zone_dnssec for the zone.

rules.threat: Without Amazon Detective, investigators have no linked behavior graph to trace an attacker's activity across accounts and services, slowing scoping of a breach and increasing dwell time.
rules.title: Enable Amazon Detective

remediate.snippet: Provision an aws_detective_graph and enable member accounts so investigations have a behavior graph.

requiredActions: Provision an Amazon Detective graph and enroll member accounts.

rules.threat: A REST API stage without execution logging at INFO/ERROR leaves no record of who invoked which method, so abuse of the API and authorization bypass attempts go unaudited.
rules.title: Enable API Gateway method execution logging

remediate.snippet: Set settings.logging_level to INFO or ERROR and metrics_enabled = true.

requiredActions: Set logging_level to INFO or ERROR in the method settings.

rules.threat: A Neptune cluster relying on default encryption without a customer KMS key lacks revocable, auditable key control over graph data, so a key compromise can't be independently contained.
rules.title: Use a customer KMS key for Neptune encryption
requiredActions: Set kms_key_arn to a customer-managed key on the Neptune cluster.

rules.threat: An ECR registry on basic (not enhanced) scanning misses OS and language-package CVEs continuously surfaced by Inspector, letting vulnerable images sit in the registry and ship to production.
rules.title: Enable enhanced ECR registry scanning

remediate.snippet: Set scan_type = "ENHANCED" with continuous scan rules covering all repositories.

requiredActions: Set scan_type to ENHANCED with continuous scanning rules covering all repositories.

rules.threat: SSM Session Manager documents without KMS encryption transmit interactive session data without an extra encryption layer, weakening protection of privileged shell sessions and any secrets typed within them.
rules.title: Encrypt SSM Session Manager sessions with KMS

remediate.snippet: In the Session-type document content set inputs.kmsKeyId to a CMK and s3EncryptionEnabled/cloudWatchEncryptionEnabled true.

requiredActions: Set kmsKeyId in the SSM Session Manager preferences document and enable log encryption.

rules.threat: A Batch job definition with a privileged container grants near-host capabilities, so an attacker who compromises the job can escape to the underlying instance and pivot into the cluster.
rules.title: Disallow privileged containers in Batch jobs

remediate.snippet: Remove privileged: true from the container_properties and grant only the specific Linux capabilities required.

requiredActions: Remove privileged from Batch container properties and use least-privilege capabilities.

rules.threat: An OpenSearch domain without audit log publishing keeps no record of index access and auth events, so an attacker reading or modifying indexed data leaves no trail for investigation.
rules.title: Publish OpenSearch audit logs

remediate.snippet: Add log_publishing_options for AUDIT_LOGS (and ES_APPLICATION_LOGS) to a CloudWatch log group.

requiredActions: Add log_publishing_options publishing AUDIT_LOGS to CloudWatch.

rules.threat: An OpenSearch domain without fine-grained access control relies solely on coarse IAM/IP rules, so any principal reaching the endpoint can query all indices, with no document/field-level authorization to limit blast radius.
rules.title: Enable OpenSearch fine-grained access control

remediate.snippet: Add advanced_security_options { enabled = true internal_user_database_enabled = ... } with a master user and role-based index access.

requiredActions: Add advanced_security_options enabling fine-grained access control with role-based index permissions.

rules.threat: An AppSync GraphQL API defaulting to API_KEY authentication ships a long-lived shared key that, once leaked, grants full schema access to any attacker who finds it.
rules.title: Avoid API key as AppSync default auth

remediate.snippet: Set authentication_type to AMAZON_COGNITO_USER_POOLS, AWS_IAM, or OPENID_CONNECT instead of API_KEY.

requiredActions: Change the AppSync default authentication_type away from API_KEY to Cognito/IAM/OIDC.

rules.threat: A WAFv2 web ACL with no logging configuration records nothing about blocked or allowed requests, so an attacker probing for evasions operates without leaving an audit trail.
rules.title: Configure WAFv2 logging (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add an AWS::WAFv2::LoggingConfiguration with ResourceArn pointing at the web ACL and LogDestinationConfigs to a Firehose/CW/S3 destination.

requiredActions: Add an AWS::WAFv2::LoggingConfiguration resource bound to the web ACL ARN.

rules.threat: A REST API stage without a backend client certificate cannot prove gateway identity to the origin, letting an attacker on the origin network forge gateway-originated requests.
rules.title: Attach a client certificate to API Gateway stages (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Create an AWS::ApiGateway::ClientCertificate and reference its id via ClientCertificateId on the stage.

requiredActions: Set ClientCertificateId on the stage referencing an AWS::ApiGateway::ClientCertificate.

rules.threat: An API Gateway method with AuthorizationType NONE is anonymous, so any internet client can invoke the backend integration directly and abuse business logic.
rules.title: Require authorization on API Gateway methods (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set AuthorizationType to AWS_IAM, COGNITO_USER_POOLS, or CUSTOM and attach an authorizer; reserve NONE for intentional public methods.

requiredActions: Set AuthorizationType away from NONE on non-public methods and attach an authorizer.

rules.threat: An API Gateway method cache without encryption stores response bodies in plaintext, exposing cached tokens and PII to anyone able to read the cache.
rules.title: Encrypt API Gateway cache data (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set CacheDataEncrypted to true in MethodSettings whenever CachingEnabled is true.

requiredActions: Set CacheDataEncrypted to true in the stage MethodSettings when caching is enabled.

rules.threat: A Cognito user pool with MfaConfiguration OFF lets a single phished or stuffed password fully compromise the account with no second factor.
rules.title: Enforce MFA on Cognito user pools (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set MfaConfiguration to ON and configure EnabledMfas (SOFTWARE_TOKEN_MFA).

requiredActions: Set MfaConfiguration to ON and enable a software token MFA method.

rules.threat: A Cognito user pool without an explicit password policy accepts weak short passwords, accelerating credential guessing against the user directory.
rules.title: Define a strong Cognito password policy (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add Policies.PasswordPolicy with MinimumLength >= 14 and RequireUppercase/Lowercase/Numbers/Symbols true.

requiredActions: Add a PasswordPolicy requiring at least 14 characters plus mixed character classes.

rules.threat: An ECS container with a writable root filesystem lets an attacker who gains code execution drop tools and persist inside the task.
rules.title: Read-only ECS container root filesystem (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set ReadonlyRootFilesystem: true in each ContainerDefinition and mount tmpfs/volumes for writable paths.

requiredActions: Set ReadonlyRootFilesystem to true in every ContainerDefinition.

rules.threat: An ECS container running privileged has near-host capabilities, letting an attacker escape the container to the underlying instance.
rules.title: Disallow privileged ECS containers (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Remove Privileged: true from ContainerDefinitions and grant only required Linux capabilities.

requiredActions: Remove Privileged: true from ECS ContainerDefinitions.

rules.threat: An ECS task using host network mode shares the instance network namespace, letting a compromised container reach loopback services and the instance metadata path of neighboring workloads.
rules.title: Avoid host network mode for ECS tasks (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Use awsvpc network mode instead of host for task isolation.

requiredActions: Set NetworkMode to awsvpc instead of host.

rules.threat: An ECS service assigning a public IP exposes tasks directly to the internet, expanding the attack surface for any vulnerable container port.
rules.title: Disable public IP on ECS services (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set AssignPublicIp to DISABLED in AwsvpcConfiguration and reach the internet via NAT.

requiredActions: Set AssignPublicIp to DISABLED in the service network configuration.

rules.threat: A Lambda without a dead-letter queue silently drops failed async invocations, erasing evidence of attacker-triggered errors.
rules.title: Configure Lambda dead-letter queue (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add DeadLetterConfig.TargetArn pointing at an SQS queue or SNS topic.

requiredActions: Add a DeadLetterConfig with a TargetArn for failed async invocations.

rules.threat: A Lambda not attached to a VPC cannot be constrained with network controls and reaches private resources only over the public internet, widening blast radius on compromise.
rules.title: Run sensitive Lambdas in a VPC (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add VpcConfig with SubnetIds and SecurityGroupIds to place the function in private subnets.

requiredActions: Add a VpcConfig with private subnets and restrictive security groups.

rules.threat: A DynamoDB table without deletion protection can be destroyed by one compromised credential, wiping production data.
rules.title: Enable DynamoDB deletion protection (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set DeletionProtectionEnabled: true on the table.

requiredActions: Set DeletionProtectionEnabled to true on production tables.

rules.threat: A bucket without Object Lock lets an attacker or compromised credential overwrite/delete objects, defeating ransomware recovery and log immutability.
rules.title: Enable S3 Object Lock for WORM buckets (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set ObjectLockEnabled: true and define an ObjectLockConfiguration retention rule for immutable buckets.

requiredActions: Set ObjectLockEnabled and an ObjectLockConfiguration retention rule on WORM buckets.

rules.threat: A CloudFront custom origin allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 or SSLv3 to the backend can be downgraded by a network attacker, exposing edge-to-origin traffic.
rules.title: Require TLS 1.2+ on CloudFront origins (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set OriginSSLProtocols to TLSv1.2 only and OriginProtocolPolicy to https-only.

requiredActions: Restrict OriginSSLProtocols to TLSv1.2 and use https-only origin protocol policy.

rules.threat: An ELBv2 listener on the HTTP protocol serves application traffic in cleartext, letting an on-path attacker read sessions and inject content.
rules.title: Use HTTPS/TLS on ELB listeners (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Use Protocol HTTPS/TLS with a Certificates list, or set the HTTP listener default action to redirect to HTTPS.

requiredActions: Use HTTPS/TLS listeners with certificates, or redirect HTTP listeners to HTTPS.

rules.threat: An ELBv2 HTTPS listener pinned to a TLS-1.0/1.1 SSL policy lets an attacker negotiate weak protocols, breaking confidentiality of client traffic.
rules.title: Use a modern ELB listener TLS policy (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set SslPolicy to a TLS 1.2+ policy such as ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06.

requiredActions: Set SslPolicy to a TLS 1.2+ (or 1.3) security policy.

rules.threat: A Firehose delivery stream without server-side encryption buffers records in plaintext, exposing in-flight log/event data.
rules.title: Encrypt Firehose delivery streams (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add DeliveryStreamEncryptionConfigurationInput with KeyType CUSTOMER_MANAGED_CMK and KeyARN.

requiredActions: Add DeliveryStreamEncryptionConfigurationInput, preferably with a customer-managed CMK.

rules.threat: A Step Functions state machine without logging records no execution detail, so abuse invoking privileged tasks leaves no audit trail.
rules.title: Enable Step Functions logging (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add LoggingConfiguration with Level ALL and a CloudWatchLogsLogGroup destination.

requiredActions: Add a LoggingConfiguration with Level ALL to a CloudWatch log group.

rules.threat: A Transfer Family server on a legacy security policy negotiates weak SFTP/FTPS ciphers, letting a network attacker downgrade file-transfer confidentiality.
rules.title: Use a modern Transfer Family security policy (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set SecurityPolicyName to a current policy version that removes weak ciphers.

requiredActions: Set SecurityPolicyName to a current Transfer Family security policy version.

rules.threat: An FSx Windows file system without a KMS key lacks customer-controlled encryption, leaving hosted shares without revocable key control.
rules.title: Encrypt FSx Windows file systems (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set KmsKeyId to a customer-managed key on the FSx file system.

requiredActions: Set KmsKeyId to a customer-managed key on the FSx file system.

rules.threat: A Backup vault without a lock lets a compromised admin shorten retention or delete recovery points, so ransomware can destroy the backups meant for recovery.
rules.title: Apply a Backup vault lock (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add a LockConfiguration with MinRetentionDays (and ChangeableForDays for compliance mode).

requiredActions: Add a LockConfiguration enforcing minimum retention.

rules.threat: A GuardDuty detector created disabled provides no threat detection, leaving reconnaissance, credential abuse, and exfiltration undetected.
rules.title: Enable the GuardDuty detector (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set Enable: true and configure DataSources/Features for S3 and malware protection.

requiredActions: Set Enable: true on the GuardDuty detector and enable data-source features.

rules.threat: An OpenSearch domain without audit log publishing keeps no record of index access, so data tampering leaves no trail.
rules.title: Publish OpenSearch audit logs (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add LogPublishingOptions for AUDIT_LOGS to a CloudWatch log group.

requiredActions: Add LogPublishingOptions publishing AUDIT_LOGS to CloudWatch.

rules.threat: An OpenSearch domain without fine-grained access control lets any principal reaching the endpoint query all indices with no document/field authorization.
rules.title: Enable OpenSearch fine-grained access control (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add AdvancedSecurityOptions with Enabled true and a master user / role-based index access.

requiredActions: Add AdvancedSecurityOptions enabling fine-grained access control.

rules.threat: An MSK cluster with no client authentication accepts connections from any client on the VPC, letting an attacker produce/consume topic data.
rules.title: Require MSK client authentication (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add ClientAuthentication enabling Tls (ACM CAs) or Sasl Iam.

requiredActions: Add a ClientAuthentication block enabling mutual TLS or SASL/IAM.

rules.threat: An MSK cluster without broker log delivery produces no broker activity record, denying responders an audit trail.
rules.title: Enable MSK broker logging (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add LoggingInfo.BrokerLogs delivering to CloudWatch, S3, or Firehose.

requiredActions: Add a LoggingInfo.BrokerLogs block delivering broker logs.

rules.threat: Glue jobs without an encrypted security configuration write logs, bookmarks, and S3 outputs unencrypted, exposing intermediate datasets.
rules.title: Encrypt Glue security configuration (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Define EncryptionConfiguration covering CloudWatch, JobBookmarks, and S3 with KMS modes.

requiredActions: Define an EncryptionConfiguration covering CloudWatch, job bookmarks, and S3 with KMS.

rules.threat: An EFS file system policy without an aws:SecureTransport deny permits NFS mounts without TLS, exposing file traffic on the network.
rules.title: Enforce in-transit encryption via EFS policy (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Attach a FileSystemPolicy with a Deny on aws:SecureTransport false to require TLS mounts.

requiredActions: Attach a FileSystemPolicy denying non-TLS access.

rules.threat: A Redshift cluster without audit logging keeps no record of connections and queries, so abuse of database access goes unaudited.
rules.title: Enable Redshift audit logging (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add LoggingProperties with a BucketName (or LogDestinationType cloudwatch) to capture connection and user activity logs.

requiredActions: Add LoggingProperties to capture connection and user-activity logs.

rules.threat: A SageMaker notebook without a KMS key stores notebook content and data on a volume lacking customer-controlled encryption.
rules.title: Encrypt SageMaker notebook storage (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set KmsKeyId to a customer-managed key on the notebook instance.

requiredActions: Set KmsKeyId to a customer-managed key on the SageMaker notebook instance.

rules.threat: An HTTP API route with AuthorizationType NONE is anonymous, letting any client invoke the backend integration and abuse business logic.
rules.title: Require authorization on HTTP API routes (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set AuthorizationType to JWT or AWS_IAM and attach an authorizer.

requiredActions: Set AuthorizationType away from NONE and attach an authorizer.

rules.threat: A Network Firewall without logging configuration records no flow/alert data, so probing or exfiltration through it leaves no trail.
rules.title: Enable Network Firewall logging (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Configure LoggingConfiguration with FLOW and ALERT LogDestinationConfigs to S3/CloudWatch.

requiredActions: Configure Network Firewall logging for FLOW and ALERT log types.

rules.threat: A DocumentDB cluster parameter group with tls disabled allows plaintext connections, exposing documents and credentials on the network.
rules.title: Enable TLS in DocumentDB parameter group (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set the tls parameter to enabled in the DocumentDB cluster parameter group.

requiredActions: Set the tls parameter to enabled in the DocumentDB cluster parameter group.

rules.threat: A default security group with ingress/egress rules silently grants access to resources that omit a group, opening an unexpected lateral path.
rules.title: Avoid open rules on security groups (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Remove ::/0 (and 0.0.0.0/0) ingress entries and scope sources to specific CIDRs or security groups.

requiredActions: Remove ::/0 and 0.0.0.0/0 ingress from security groups; scope to specific sources.

rules.threat: An AppSync GraphQL API defaulting to API_KEY auth ships a long-lived shared key that grants full schema access if leaked.
rules.title: Avoid API key as AppSync default auth (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set AuthenticationType to AMAZON_COGNITO_USER_POOLS, AWS_IAM, or OPENID_CONNECT.

requiredActions: Change AuthenticationType away from API_KEY to Cognito/IAM/OIDC.

rules.threat: An EMR cluster without a security configuration runs with no encryption or Kerberos, letting an attacker on the cluster network read HDFS and inter-node traffic.
rules.title: Attach an EMR security configuration (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Reference an AWS::EMR::SecurityConfiguration (at-rest, in-transit, Kerberos) via SecurityConfiguration.

requiredActions: Reference an EMR SecurityConfiguration providing encryption and Kerberos.

rules.threat: An Athena workgroup without result encryption stores query output in S3 in plaintext, exposing regulated query results to anyone reading the bucket.
rules.title: Encrypt Athena workgroup results (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add ResultConfiguration.EncryptionConfiguration with SSE_KMS and enforce the workgroup configuration.

requiredActions: Add ResultConfiguration.EncryptionConfiguration using SSE_KMS and enforce workgroup config.

rules.threat: A CodePipeline using the default artifact key lacks customer-controlled encryption of build artifacts that may contain secrets or proprietary code.
rules.title: Encrypt CodePipeline artifacts with KMS (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add EncryptionKey (Id + Type KMS) to the ArtifactStore configuration.

requiredActions: Add an EncryptionKey referencing a customer-managed KMS key to the ArtifactStore.

rules.threat: A Timestream database without a customer KMS key relies on default encryption, leaving time-series telemetry without revocable key control.
rules.title: Encrypt Timestream databases with KMS (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set KmsKeyId to a customer-managed key on the Timestream database.

requiredActions: Set KmsKeyId to a customer-managed key on the Timestream database.

rules.threat: A legacy Elasticsearch domain without audit log publishing keeps no record of index access, so data tampering goes unaudited.
rules.title: Publish Elasticsearch audit logs (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Add LogPublishingOptions for AUDIT_LOGS to a CloudWatch log group.

requiredActions: Add LogPublishingOptions publishing AUDIT_LOGS to CloudWatch.

rules.threat: An unencrypted EBS volume in a launch template propagates to unencrypted snapshots that can be copied and restored with data readable, enabling exfiltration.
rules.title: Encrypt EBS volumes in launch templates (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: In LaunchTemplateData BlockDeviceMappings Ebs set Encrypted: true with a KmsKeyId.

requiredActions: Set Encrypted: true (with KmsKeyId) on EBS block device mappings in the launch template.

rules.threat: A DMS replication instance that is publicly accessible exposes the replication endpoint to the internet, giving attackers a path to intercept or tamper with in-flight migrated data.
rules.title: Disable public access on DMS instances (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set PubliclyAccessible: false and place the instance in private subnets.

requiredActions: Set PubliclyAccessible to false on the DMS replication instance.

rules.threat: An RDS instance pinned to the expiring rds-ca-2019 bundle forces clients onto an old CA; on expiry connections fail or downgrade, opening a window to impersonate the DB endpoint.
rules.title: Use a current RDS CA bundle (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set CACertificateIdentifier to a current bundle such as rds-ca-rsa2048-g1.

requiredActions: Set CACertificateIdentifier to a current RDS CA bundle.

rules.threat: An EKS public endpoint open to 0.0.0.0/0 exposes the Kubernetes API server to the whole internet for auth brute force and control-plane exploits.
rules.title: Restrict EKS public endpoint CIDRs (CloudFormation)

remediate.snippet: Set PublicAccessCidrs to a tight allowlist, or disable the public endpoint and use private access.

requiredActions: Replace 0.0.0.0/0 in PublicAccessCidrs with an allowlist, or disable the public endpoint.

rules.threat: An App Runner service left publicly reachable lets attackers reach the container directly over the internet, bypassing VPC and WAF controls and probing the app for vulnerabilities.
rules.title: Make App Runner service ingress private via VPC ingress connection
requiredActions: Set is_publicly_accessible = false inside network_configuration.ingress_configuration.

rules.threat: Without a customer-managed KMS key, App Runner stores copied source and build artifacts under AWS-owned keys you cannot audit or revoke, weakening blast-radius control after a key-exposure incident.
rules.title: Encrypt App Runner service with a customer-managed KMS key

remediate.snippet: Add an encryption_configuration block with kms_key set to a CMK ARN.

requiredActions: Add encryption_configuration { kms_key = <cmk_arn> } to the service.

rules.threat: A Grafana workspace without an authentication provider configured exposes dashboards and data-source credentials to anyone who reaches the endpoint, enabling credential theft and lateral movement into backing data stores.
rules.title: Require an authentication provider on Grafana workspaces

remediate.snippet: Set authentication_providers = ["AWS_SSO"] or ["SAML"].

requiredActions: Set authentication_providers to AWS_SSO and/or SAML.

rules.threat: An Airflow web server exposed with PUBLIC_ONLY access lets attackers reach the UI from the internet and attempt credential stuffing or DAG-trigger abuse to run arbitrary code in the environment.
rules.title: Set MWAA web server access mode to PRIVATE_ONLY
requiredActions: Set webserver_access_mode = "PRIVATE_ONLY".

rules.threat: Without a customer KMS key, MWAA stores DAGs, connection secrets, and logs under AWS-managed keys, removing your ability to revoke access during a compromise of the Airflow environment.
rules.title: Encrypt MWAA environment with a customer-managed KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key to a CMK ARN on the MWAA environment.

rules.threat: An EMR cluster without termination protection can be destroyed by a compromised credential or accidental API call, wiping running jobs and the data on local HDFS storage as a denial-of-service.
rules.title: Enable termination protection on EMR clusters
requiredActions: Set termination_protection = true on the EMR cluster.

rules.threat: A Glue job without a security configuration writes job bookmarks, CloudWatch logs, and S3 output in plaintext, exposing processed data and pipeline state to anyone with read access to those targets.
rules.title: Attach a security configuration to Glue jobs
requiredActions: Set security_configuration on the Glue job to an encrypting security configuration.

rules.threat: An unencrypted Glue Data Catalog stores table metadata, including connection passwords, in plaintext, letting any reader harvest credentials to backing databases.
rules.title: Enable at-rest encryption on the Glue Data Catalog

remediate.snippet: Add encryption_at_rest { catalog_encryption_mode = "SSE-KMS" } and connection_password_encryption.

requiredActions: Configure encryption_at_rest with catalog_encryption_mode = SSE-KMS.

rules.threat: Without connection password encryption, Glue catalog connection secrets are retrievable in plaintext via the API, handing attackers direct credentials to JDBC and other data sources.
rules.title: Encrypt Glue catalog connection passwords

remediate.snippet: Add connection_password_encryption { return_connection_password_encrypted = true, aws_kms_key_id = <arn> }.

requiredActions: Enable connection_password_encryption with return_connection_password_encrypted = true.

rules.threat: A Kendra index without a customer KMS key stores indexed documents and query data under AWS-owned keys, so sensitive enterprise content cannot be cryptographically revoked after a breach.
rules.title: Encrypt Kendra index with a customer-managed KMS key

remediate.snippet: Add server_side_encryption_configuration { kms_key_id = <arn> }.

requiredActions: Add server_side_encryption_configuration with a KMS key id to the Kendra index.

rules.threat: A Comprehend custom entity recognizer without volume KMS encryption stores training data on unencrypted ML compute storage, exposing sensitive corpora if the underlying volume is accessed.
rules.title: Encrypt Comprehend entity recognizer compute volume with KMS
requiredActions: Set volume_kms_key_id on the entity recognizer.

rules.threat: An IoT topic rule lacking error-handling and logging can silently drop or misroute device telemetry, masking tampering and giving attackers cover to inject spoofed device messages undetected.
rules.title: Configure error action on IoT topic rules

remediate.snippet: Add an error_action block routing failed messages to a monitored target.

requiredActions: Add an error_action block to the IoT topic rule.

rules.threat: An IoT policy granting iot:* on resource * lets any compromised device publish, subscribe, and connect across every topic, turning one cracked device into full fleet control.
rules.title: Remove wildcard actions from IoT policies

remediate.snippet: Scope the IoT policy to specific iot: actions and ARNs.

requiredActions: Replace iot:* with the minimal set of required IoT actions.

rules.threat: An MSK Serverless cluster without client authentication accepts unauthenticated broker connections, letting any network-adjacent attacker read and write Kafka topics carrying sensitive event data.
rules.title: Require IAM client authentication on MSK Serverless

remediate.snippet: Add client_authentication { sasl { iam { enabled = true } } }.

requiredActions: Add a client_authentication block enabling SASL/IAM.

rules.threat: An EventBridge Scheduler schedule without a customer KMS key encrypts target payloads (which can carry secrets and parameters) under AWS-owned keys, preventing revocation after credential exposure.
rules.title: Encrypt EventBridge Scheduler schedules with a customer KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_arn on the scheduler schedule.

rules.threat: A DataSync task without CloudWatch log configuration runs data transfers with no audit trail, letting an attacker who hijacks the task exfiltrate data between locations undetected.
rules.title: Enable CloudWatch logging on DataSync tasks
requiredActions: Set cloudwatch_log_group_arn on the DataSync task.

rules.threat: An NFS file share on Storage Gateway without KMS encryption writes objects to the backing S3 bucket unencrypted, exposing on-prem-synced data to anyone with bucket read access.
rules.title: Enable KMS encryption on Storage Gateway NFS file shares
requiredActions: Set kms_encrypted = true and supply kms_key_arn on the file share.

rules.threat: An FSx for NetApp ONTAP file system without a KMS key uses AWS-owned key encryption, removing your ability to revoke access to shared file data after a compromise.
rules.title: Encrypt FSx ONTAP file systems with a customer KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_id on the FSx ONTAP file system.

rules.threat: An FSx for OpenZFS file system without a customer KMS key stores file data under AWS-owned keys, weakening blast-radius control if credentials leak.
rules.title: Encrypt FSx OpenZFS file systems with a customer KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_id on the FSx OpenZFS file system.

rules.threat: A WorkSpaces directory with default internet access auto-assigns public IPs to workspaces, exposing employee desktops to direct inbound internet probing and exploitation.
rules.title: Disable default internet access on WorkSpaces directories
requiredActions: Set enable_internet_access = false in workspace_creation_properties.

rules.threat: An AppStream fleet with default internet access places streaming instances directly on the internet, broadening the attack surface for the streamed applications and underlying OS.
rules.title: Disable default internet access on AppStream fleets
requiredActions: Set enable_default_internet_access = false on the fleet.

rules.threat: An Amazon Connect instance with contact flow logs disabled provides no audit trail for agent and customer interactions, hiding social-engineering and data-exfiltration activity within the contact center.
rules.title: Enable contact flow logs on Amazon Connect instances
requiredActions: Set contact_flow_logs_enabled = true on the Connect instance.

rules.threat: An SESv2 configuration set with Optional TLS allows email to be sent over cleartext SMTP, letting a network attacker intercept message contents and recipient data.
rules.title: Require TLS on SESv2 configuration sets
requiredActions: Set delivery_options.tls_policy = "REQUIRE".

rules.threat: A legacy SES configuration set with Optional TLS permits plaintext message delivery, exposing email contents to on-path interception.
rules.title: Require TLS on SES configuration set delivery options
requiredActions: Set delivery_options.tls_policy = "Require".

rules.threat: X-Ray trace data encrypted with the default key cannot be cryptographically revoked; an attacker reading traces can map internal service topology, endpoints, and request metadata for targeting.
rules.title: Encrypt X-Ray traces with a customer-managed KMS key
requiredActions: Set type = "KMS" and supply key_id on the X-Ray encryption config.

rules.threat: An Image Builder distribution configuration that shares AMIs with all accounts publishes potentially secret-laden machine images to every AWS account, leaking baked-in credentials and proprietary software.
rules.title: Do not publicly share Image Builder AMIs

remediate.snippet: Remove "all" from launch_permission user_groups; share only with specific account ids.

requiredActions: Remove "all" from launch_permission.user_groups in the distribution configuration.

rules.threat: A VPC endpoint with a wildcard principal policy lets resources from any account use the endpoint, enabling data exfiltration through the interface to cross-account services.
rules.title: Restrict VPC endpoint policy principals

remediate.snippet: Scope the endpoint policy to specific account principals and resources.

requiredActions: Replace the wildcard Principal in the VPC endpoint policy with specific principals.

rules.threat: An interface VPC endpoint without private DNS forces clients to public service DNS, routing traffic over the internet gateway instead of the private link and exposing it to interception.
rules.title: Enable private DNS on interface VPC endpoints
requiredActions: Set private_dns_enabled = true on the interface endpoint.

rules.threat: Without resolver query logging, malicious DNS lookups from compromised hosts (data exfiltration via DNS, C2 beaconing) leave no record, blinding incident response.
rules.title: Enable Route 53 Resolver query logging
requiredActions: Set destination_arn on the resolver query log config.

rules.threat: A GuardDuty organization configuration that does not auto-enable for new accounts leaves freshly created accounts unmonitored, giving attackers a blind spot to operate in.
rules.title: Auto-enable GuardDuty for new organization accounts
requiredActions: Set auto_enable_organization_members = "ALL".

rules.threat: With GuardDuty Kubernetes audit log monitoring disabled, EKS control-plane attacks like privilege escalation and anonymous API access go undetected.
rules.title: Enable GuardDuty EKS audit log monitoring
requiredActions: Set status = "ENABLED" on the EKS_AUDIT_LOGS GuardDuty feature.

rules.threat: An SSM parameter holding a secret as a plaintext String type stores it unencrypted and readable by anyone with ssm:GetParameter, leaking credentials directly.
rules.title: Store sensitive SSM parameters as SecureString
requiredActions: Set type = "SecureString" for parameters holding secrets.

rules.threat: A secret configured to force-delete immediately (recovery_window_in_days = 0) lets an attacker permanently destroy credentials with no recovery period, enabling irreversible sabotage.
rules.title: Set a non-zero recovery window on Secrets Manager secrets
requiredActions: Set recovery_window_in_days to a value of 7 or greater.

rules.threat: An IAM role with an oversized max session duration issues long-lived assumed-role credentials; if stolen, they remain valid for hours, extending an attacker's window before expiry.
rules.title: Limit IAM role maximum session duration
requiredActions: Reduce max_session_duration to 3600 seconds where the workload allows.

rules.threat: An IAM role whose assume-role trust policy uses Principal AWS "*" can be assumed by any AWS account, letting an external attacker take the role and inherit all its permissions.
rules.title: Forbid wildcard principals in IAM role trust policies

remediate.snippet: Restrict assume_role_policy Principal to specific account or service ARNs with conditions.

requiredActions: Replace the wildcard AWS principal in assume_role_policy with explicit trusted principals.

rules.threat: A DMS endpoint with ssl_mode none replicates database data over an unencrypted connection, letting a network attacker capture migrated records and credentials in transit.
rules.title: Require SSL on DMS endpoints
requiredActions: Set ssl_mode to require or stronger on the DMS endpoint.

rules.threat: A Lambda function with environment variables but no customer KMS key encrypts those variables (often holding secrets) under an AWS-managed key, so access cannot be cryptographically revoked after exposure.
rules.title: Encrypt Lambda environment variables with a customer KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_arn on the Lambda function.

rules.threat: A Cognito identity pool allowing unauthenticated identities hands temporary AWS credentials to anonymous callers, who can then invoke any action the unauth IAM role permits.
rules.title: Disable unauthenticated identities on Cognito identity pools
requiredActions: Set allow_unauthenticated_identities = false unless anonymous access is required.

rules.threat: A SageMaker model without network isolation lets inference containers make outbound calls, enabling a poisoned or compromised model to exfiltrate training data or call internal services.
rules.title: Enable network isolation on SageMaker models
requiredActions: Set enable_network_isolation = true on the SageMaker model.

rules.threat: A SageMaker Studio domain without a KMS key stores user notebooks and EFS home directories under AWS-managed keys, preventing revocation of access to potentially sensitive ML code and data.
rules.title: Encrypt SageMaker domain storage with a customer KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_id on the SageMaker domain.

rules.threat: A SageMaker domain with PublicInternetOnly app network access lets Studio apps reach the internet directly, providing an exfiltration path for notebook data.
rules.title: Set SageMaker domain app network access to VpcOnly
requiredActions: Set app_network_access_type = "VpcOnly".

rules.threat: An OpenSearch Serverless security policy of type encryption that relies on AWS-owned keys (no customer KMS) cannot be revoked, leaving indexed data exposed after a key incident.
rules.title: Use a customer KMS key in OpenSearch Serverless encryption policy

remediate.snippet: Define an encryption security policy referencing a customer KMS key in its policy document.

requiredActions: Create an encryption-type security policy bound to a customer KMS key.

rules.threat: A Keyspaces (Cassandra) table using AWS-owned key encryption cannot have its key revoked, leaving stored wide-column data exposed after credential or key compromise.
rules.title: Encrypt Keyspaces tables with a customer-managed KMS key

remediate.snippet: Add encryption_specification { type = "CUSTOMER_MANAGED_KMS_KEY", kms_key_identifier = <arn> }.

requiredActions: Add encryption_specification with CUSTOMER_MANAGED_KMS_KEY to the table.

rules.threat: A Keyspaces table without point-in-time recovery cannot be restored after malicious or accidental deletion, making data destruction permanent.
rules.title: Enable point-in-time recovery on Keyspaces tables

remediate.snippet: Add point_in_time_recovery { status = "ENABLED" }.

requiredActions: Add point_in_time_recovery { status = "ENABLED" }.

rules.threat: An ACM Private CA whose Subordinate-issuing template is unconstrained can mint certificates for any domain, enabling an attacker who reaches the CA to impersonate internal services.
rules.title: Set usage mode on ACM Private CA
requiredActions: Set usage_mode on the private CA to match its intended use.

rules.threat: A network ACL rule allowing 0.0.0.0/0 inbound to admin ports exposes SSH/RDP to the entire internet, inviting brute-force and exploitation of management interfaces.
rules.title: Block open admin ports in network ACL rules

remediate.snippet: Restrict the cidr_block to known admin source ranges.

requiredActions: Replace 0.0.0.0/0 on admin-port NACL rules with specific source CIDRs.

rules.threat: A route sending 0.0.0.0/0 to an internet gateway in a subnet intended to be private turns it into a public subnet, exposing every instance there to inbound internet traffic.
rules.title: Avoid default routes to internet gateway on private route tables

remediate.snippet: Route private-subnet egress through a NAT gateway, not an internet gateway.

requiredActions: Confirm the route belongs to a public subnet; otherwise route via NAT gateway.

rules.threat: Adding an internet gateway default route to the VPC default route table silently makes all subnets that use it public, exposing instances that operators assumed were private.
rules.title: Keep the default route table free of internet gateway routes

remediate.snippet: Use explicit route tables for public subnets; leave the default route table local-only.

requiredActions: Remove the igw route from the default route table and use dedicated public route tables.

rules.threat: A Global Accelerator without flow logs provides no record of client connections, so DDoS reconnaissance and abuse of the accelerator's static anycast IPs go unobserved.
rules.title: Enable flow logs on Global Accelerator
requiredActions: Set attributes.flow_logs_enabled = true and supply flow_logs_s3_bucket.

rules.threat: An EventBridge custom event bus without a KMS key encrypts event payloads (which can carry sensitive fields) under AWS-owned keys, so access cannot be revoked after compromise.
rules.title: Encrypt EventBridge event bus with a customer KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_identifier on the event bus.

rules.threat: An EventBridge Pipe without a KMS key encrypts in-flight buffered data under AWS-owned keys, leaving the pipe's transient payloads unrevocable after a key incident.
rules.title: Encrypt EventBridge Pipes with a customer KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_identifier on the pipe.

rules.threat: A Shield Advanced protection without an associated health check leaves Route 53 failover and DDoS detection blind, slowing mitigation during a volumetric attack.
rules.title: Configure Shield protection with health checks

remediate.snippet: Associate the Shield protection with a health-checked resource and aws_shield_protection_health_check_association.

requiredActions: Associate a health check with the Shield protection.

rules.threat: A Security Lake data lake without a customer KMS key encrypts aggregated security logs under AWS-owned keys, so the central trove of security telemetry cannot be access-revoked.
rules.title: Encrypt Security Lake with a customer KMS key

remediate.snippet: Add a configuration block with encryption_configuration { kms_key_id = <arn> }.

requiredActions: Configure encryption_configuration with a customer KMS key in the data lake.

rules.threat: An ECS task definition mounting Docker volumes without encryption can persist sensitive scratch data in plaintext on the host, recoverable by anyone with host access.
rules.title: Avoid host bind mounts in ECS task definitions

remediate.snippet: Replace host bind mounts with encrypted EFS volumes or remove them.

requiredActions: Remove host_path bind mounts; use encrypted volume configurations.

rules.threat: A Verified Access instance without access logging records no record of zero-trust authorization decisions, hiding unauthorized access attempts and policy bypass from defenders.
rules.title: Enable logging on Verified Access instances

remediate.snippet: Configure access_logs to a CloudWatch log group, S3 bucket, or Kinesis stream.

requiredActions: Configure an access_logs destination on the Verified Access logging configuration.

rules.threat: A Data Lifecycle Manager policy copying snapshots cross-region without enforcing encryption can produce unencrypted snapshot copies, exposing volume data in the destination region.
rules.title: Ensure DLM cross-region snapshot copies are encrypted

remediate.snippet: Set encrypted = true in cross_region_copy_rule and provide cmk_arn.

requiredActions: Set encrypted = true in DLM cross_region_copy_rule blocks.

rules.threat: A Signer signing profile without a constrained signature validity period issues long-lived code-signing material, widening the window an attacker can abuse a leaked signing identity.
rules.title: Constrain Signer signing profile validity

remediate.snippet: Add a signature_validity_period block with a bounded value/type.

requiredActions: Add a signature_validity_period to the signing profile.

rules.threat: A MemoryDB cluster without at-rest KMS encryption stores cached data and snapshots in plaintext, exposing it if the underlying storage or snapshot is accessed.
rules.title: Encrypt MemoryDB clusters at rest with a customer KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_arn on the MemoryDB cluster.

rules.threat: An MQ broker without audit logging keeps no record of administrative actions on the message broker, masking malicious queue manipulation and configuration tampering.
rules.title: Enable audit logging on MQ brokers

remediate.snippet: Add a logs { audit = true, general = true } block.

requiredActions: Add a logs block enabling audit and general logging.

rules.threat: An MQ broker with auto minor version upgrades disabled misses security patches, leaving known broker CVEs exploitable.
rules.title: Enable auto minor version upgrades on MQ brokers
requiredActions: Set auto_minor_version_upgrade = true on the MQ broker.

rules.threat: A CodeBuild project with privileged_mode enabled grants the build container root-equivalent Docker daemon access; a malicious dependency or PR can escape the build to attack the host and steal CI credentials.
rules.title: Disable privileged mode on CodeBuild projects

remediate.snippet: Set privileged_mode = false unless Docker-in-Docker is strictly required and isolated.

requiredActions: Set privileged_mode = false in the CodeBuild environment block where possible.

rules.threat: A Neptune DB cluster parameter group with audit logging disabled keeps no record of graph queries, hiding data exfiltration and injection against the graph database.
rules.title: Enable audit logging in Neptune parameter groups

remediate.snippet: Set the neptune_enable_audit_log parameter value to 1.

requiredActions: Set neptune_enable_audit_log parameter to "1".

rules.threat: An ElastiCache cluster sharing a subnet/SG with internet exposure can be reached by attackers who then read cached session tokens and data; lacking a private subnet group widens this risk.
rules.title: Place ElastiCache clusters in a subnet group
requiredActions: Set subnet_group_name to a private subnet group on the cluster.

rules.threat: A Redshift Serverless workgroup with public access enabled exposes the data warehouse endpoint to the internet, inviting credential attacks and direct querying of analytics data.
rules.title: Disable public access on Redshift Serverless workgroups
requiredActions: Set publicly_accessible = false on the workgroup.

rules.threat: A DocumentDB elastic cluster without a customer KMS key encrypts document data under AWS-owned keys, removing the ability to revoke access after a key incident.
rules.title: Encrypt DocumentDB elastic clusters with a customer KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_id on the DocumentDB elastic cluster.

rules.threat: A Transfer Family server without structured logging keeps no record of SFTP/FTPS file transfers, hiding bulk data exfiltration over the managed transfer endpoint.
rules.title: Enable structured logging on Transfer Family servers

remediate.snippet: Set structured_log_destinations to a CloudWatch log group ARN.

requiredActions: Set structured_log_destinations on the Transfer server.

rules.threat: An EKS managed node group exposing SSH without restricting source security groups opens worker nodes to internet brute-force, leading to node compromise and pod escape.
rules.title: Restrict SSH access on EKS node groups

remediate.snippet: If remote_access is set, restrict source_security_group_ids; otherwise omit SSH.

requiredActions: Restrict source_security_group_ids on remote_access or remove SSH access.

rules.threat: A Kinesis stream at the minimum retention loses records quickly, so a slow exfiltration or tampering campaign rolls off before detection and forensic replay is impossible.
rules.title: Increase Kinesis stream retention period
requiredActions: Set retention_period to an appropriate value (e.g. 168).

rules.threat: Bedrock without model invocation logging keeps no record of prompts and completions, hiding prompt-injection abuse, data exfiltration via prompts, and cost-amplification attacks.
rules.title: Enable Bedrock model invocation logging

remediate.snippet: Add logging_config with cloudwatch_config or s3_config destinations.

requiredActions: Add a logging_config block routing invocations to CloudWatch or S3.

rules.threat: A FinSpace environment without a customer KMS key encrypts financial datasets under AWS-owned keys, so highly sensitive market data cannot be access-revoked after compromise.
rules.title: Encrypt FinSpace environments with a customer KMS key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_id on the FinSpace kx environment.

rules.threat: An MSK Connect connector without log delivery keeps no record of connector activity, hiding misuse that streams Kafka data to unauthorized sinks.
rules.title: Enable log delivery on MSK Connect connectors

remediate.snippet: Add a log_delivery block with a CloudWatch, S3, or Firehose destination.

requiredActions: Add a log_delivery block to the connector.

rules.threat: A Verified Access group without an attached access policy defaults to denying or allowing without context evaluation, potentially granting zero-trust access without enforcing identity conditions.
rules.title: Attach an access policy to Verified Access groups

remediate.snippet: Set policy_document with a Cedar policy enforcing identity conditions.

requiredActions: Set policy_document on the Verified Access group.

rules.threat: Inline secrets stored unencrypted in an AppConfig hosted configuration version are distributed in plaintext to every consuming application, leaking credentials broadly.
rules.title: Avoid plaintext secrets in AppConfig configuration versions

remediate.snippet: Reference Secrets Manager/SSM SecureString instead of inlining secrets.

requiredActions: Remove inline secrets from the AppConfig content; reference Secrets Manager.

rules.threat: Legacy WAF Regional without logging gives no visibility into blocked or allowed requests, so web attacks slip past unexamined and tuning is impossible.
rules.title: Enable logging on WAF Regional web ACLs

remediate.snippet: Add a logging_configuration block with a Firehose ARN, or migrate to WAFv2.

requiredActions: Add logging_configuration to the WAF Regional web ACL or migrate to WAFv2.

rules.threat: An EC2 instance without detailed monitoring delays anomaly detection on a compromised host, lengthening the time an attacker operates before metrics surface unusual CPU or network activity.
rules.title: Enable detailed monitoring on EC2 instances
requiredActions: Set monitoring = true on the EC2 instance.

rules.threat: A Service Catalog portfolio shared across the entire organization can propagate misconfigured or backdoored provisioning templates to every account, scaling a single tampering into org-wide compromise.
rules.title: Scope Service Catalog portfolio shares

remediate.snippet: Share to specific account principals rather than the whole organization where possible.

requiredActions: Limit principal_id/type to specific trusted accounts.

rules.threat: A Chime Voice Connector with encryption disabled carries SIP/RTP voice traffic in cleartext, letting a network attacker eavesdrop on or hijack calls.
rules.title: Enable encryption on Chime Voice Connectors
requiredActions: Set require_encryption = true on the Chime Voice Connector.

rules.threat: Object ACLs left enabled let cross-account uploaders retain ownership and grant public/cross-account read on objects the bucket owner cannot fully control.
rules.title: Enforce bucket-owner object ownership to disable ACLs

remediate.snippet: In the rule { } block set object_ownership = "BucketOwnerEnforced" to disable ACLs entirely.

requiredActions: Set object_ownership to BucketOwnerEnforced in the aws_s3_bucket_ownership_controls rule block

rules.threat: A canned ACL of public-read or public-read-write exposes bucket contents to the entire internet for download or anonymous overwrite.
rules.title: Do not assign a public canned ACL to S3 buckets
requiredActions: Change the canned acl to private

rules.threat: An S3 access point without public-ACL blocking can re-expose a private bucket through a public access point ACL.
rules.title: Block public ACLs on S3 access points

remediate.snippet: Add a public_access_block_configuration block with block_public_acls = true, ignore_public_acls = true, block_public_policy = true, restrict_public_buckets = true.

requiredActions: Set block_public_acls = true inside public_access_block_configuration on the access point

rules.threat: Without restrict_public_buckets, an access point can serve a bucket that has a public policy, bypassing intended isolation.
rules.title: Restrict public buckets on S3 access points

remediate.snippet: Set restrict_public_buckets = true inside public_access_block_configuration.

requiredActions: Set restrict_public_buckets = true on the access point public access block

rules.threat: An inline role policy granting Action "*" hands the assuming principal full administrative control, enabling immediate privilege escalation if the role is compromised.
rules.title: Avoid wildcard actions in inline IAM role policies

remediate.snippet: Replace Action "*" with the explicit minimal set of API actions the role requires.

requiredActions: Scope the inline role policy to specific actions instead of "*"

rules.threat: An inline user policy with Action "*" makes a single leaked access key equivalent to root, letting an attacker perform any account operation.
rules.title: Avoid wildcard actions in inline IAM user policies

remediate.snippet: Replace Action "*" with explicit scoped actions; prefer attaching managed policies via groups.

requiredActions: Scope the inline user policy to specific actions

rules.threat: An inline group policy with Action "*" silently grants every member full admin rights, widening blast radius across all users in the group.
rules.title: Avoid wildcard actions in inline IAM group policies

remediate.snippet: Replace Action "*" with explicit scoped actions in the group policy document.

requiredActions: Scope the inline group policy to specific actions

rules.threat: An SCP that allows Action "*" on Resource "*" provides no guardrail, leaving member accounts free to perform any action including disabling security controls.
rules.title: Avoid permissive allow-all Service Control Policies

remediate.snippet: Replace the blanket allow with explicit deny guardrails (e.g. deny disabling CloudTrail/GuardDuty, deny region usage).

requiredActions: Rewrite the SCP to enforce deny guardrails rather than allowing all actions

rules.threat: Aurora PostgreSQL clusters that do not force SSL accept plaintext connections, allowing credential and data interception on the wire.
rules.title: Force SSL via Aurora cluster parameter group

remediate.snippet: Add a parameter { name = "rds.force_ssl" value = "1" } block to the cluster parameter group.

requiredActions: Add a parameter block setting rds.force_ssl = 1

rules.threat: A PostgreSQL parameter group without rds.force_ssl permits unencrypted client sessions, exposing data to network sniffing.
rules.title: Force SSL via DB parameter group

remediate.snippet: Add parameter { name = "rds.force_ssl" value = "1" } to enforce TLS.

requiredActions: Add a parameter block setting rds.force_ssl = 1

rules.threat: Snapshots without copied tags lose ownership and data-classification context, weakening governance and breaking tag-based access controls on restored data.
rules.title: Copy tags to snapshots on Aurora clusters
requiredActions: Set copy_tags_to_snapshot = true

rules.threat: Aurora clusters that do not export engine logs to CloudWatch leave query, error, and audit activity invisible, hindering breach detection and forensics.
rules.title: Export Aurora cluster logs to CloudWatch

remediate.snippet: Set enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports = ["audit","error","slowquery"] (or ["postgresql"]) on the cluster.

requiredActions: Set enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports to the relevant engine log types

rules.threat: Performance Insights data without a KMS key is stored under an AWS-owned key, reducing tenant control and auditability of sensitive query metrics.
rules.title: Encrypt RDS Performance Insights with a CMK

remediate.snippet: Set performance_insights_enabled = true and performance_insights_kms_key_id to a customer-managed KMS key ARN.
