requiredActions: Set performance_insights_kms_key_id to a CMK ARN

rules.threat: An ElastiCache user configured with no-password authentication allows unauthenticated access to the cache, exposing cached secrets and session data.
rules.title: Require authentication for ElastiCache users

remediate.snippet: Set no_password_required = false and configure passwords or an authentication_mode block with type "password"/"iam".

requiredActions: Disable no_password_required and configure password or IAM auth

rules.threat: ECS Exec sessions without configured logging let operators (or an attacker who pivots through them) run interactive commands in containers with no audit trail.
rules.title: Configure logging for ECS Exec on clusters

remediate.snippet: Add a configuration { execute_command_configuration { logging = "OVERRIDE" log_configuration {...} } } block.

requiredActions: Add execute_command_configuration with logging enabled

rules.threat: Leaving preserve = true on an EKS add-on keeps add-on resources after cluster deletion, orphaning IAM/network resources an attacker could later abuse.
rules.title: Do not preserve EKS add-ons on deletion
requiredActions: Set preserve = false on the EKS add-on

rules.threat: A response headers policy lacking Strict-Transport-Security lets downgrade and SSL-strip attacks force clients onto plaintext HTTP.
rules.title: Set HSTS in CloudFront response headers policy

remediate.snippet: Add security_headers_config { strict_transport_security { access_control_max_age_sec = 31536000 include_subdomains = true preload = true override = true } }.

requiredActions: Add a strict_transport_security block to the response headers policy

rules.threat: Account-wide EC2 serial console access provides an out-of-band path into instances that bypasses SSH key controls and network ACLs.
rules.title: Disable account-wide EC2 serial console access
requiredActions: Set enabled = false for serial console access

rules.threat: A default network ACL with a 0.0.0.0/0 ingress rule exposes every subnet associated with it to unrestricted inbound traffic.
rules.title: Avoid open ingress on the default network ACL

remediate.snippet: Remove the 0.0.0.0/0 ingress rule or scope it to specific trusted CIDRs.

requiredActions: Remove or restrict the 0.0.0.0/0 ingress rule on the default NACL

rules.threat: A standalone VPC security group ingress rule with cidr_ipv4 0.0.0.0/0 exposes the attached resource's ports to the entire internet.
rules.title: Avoid 0.0.0.0/0 on VPC security group ingress rules

remediate.snippet: Scope cidr_ipv4 to specific trusted ranges instead of 0.0.0.0/0.

requiredActions: Restrict cidr_ipv4 to specific CIDRs

rules.threat: A VPC security group ingress rule with cidr_ipv6 ::/0 opens the resource to the entire IPv6 internet, often overlooked compared to IPv4.
rules.title: Avoid ::/0 on VPC security group ingress rules

remediate.snippet: Scope cidr_ipv6 to specific trusted ranges instead of ::/0.

requiredActions: Restrict cidr_ipv6 to specific CIDRs

rules.threat: Auto-accepting shared attachments on a Transit Gateway lets any account it is shared with silently connect its VPCs, creating unsanctioned lateral network paths.
rules.title: Disable auto-accept of shared Transit Gateway attachments
requiredActions: Set auto_accept_shared_attachments to disable

rules.threat: A Client VPN endpoint without connection logging gives no record of who connected, from where, or when, blinding incident response on remote-access compromise.
rules.title: Enable connection logging on Client VPN endpoints

remediate.snippet: Add connection_log_options { enabled = true cloudwatch_log_group = ... }.

requiredActions: Add connection_log_options with enabled = true

rules.threat: Granting AMI launch permission to group "all" makes the image and any embedded secrets or proprietary software publicly launchable by every AWS account.
rules.title: Do not share AMIs publicly via launch permission

remediate.snippet: Share the AMI to specific account_id values instead of group = "all".

requiredActions: Replace group = "all" with explicit account_id sharing

rules.threat: A firewall policy whose stateful default action is aws:pass forwards unmatched traffic, undermining the deny-by-default posture a network firewall is meant to enforce.
rules.title: Use a drop default on Network Firewall stateful policy

remediate.snippet: Set stateful_default_actions = ["aws:drop_strict","aws:alert_strict"] and stateful_engine_options { rule_order = "STRICT_ORDER" }.

requiredActions: Set stateful default action to drop instead of pass

rules.threat: A flow log capturing only ACCEPT or only REJECT traffic misses half the network record, leaving gaps that hide reconnaissance or exfiltration.
rules.title: Capture ALL traffic in VPC flow logs
requiredActions: Set traffic_type = "ALL"

rules.threat: A Redshift Serverless namespace without a customer-managed KMS key stores warehouse data under an AWS-owned key, removing tenant control over encryption and key rotation.
rules.title: Encrypt Redshift Serverless namespace with a CMK

remediate.snippet: Set kms_key_id to a customer-managed KMS key ARN on the namespace.

requiredActions: Set kms_key_id to a CMK ARN

rules.threat: A Redshift Serverless namespace without log exports omits user-activity, connection, and audit logs, leaving query abuse and credential misuse undetectable.
rules.title: Export Redshift Serverless audit logs

remediate.snippet: Set log_exports = ["userlog","connectionlog","useractivitylog"] on the namespace.

requiredActions: Set log_exports to the relevant audit log types

rules.threat: A DAX cluster with NONE endpoint encryption serves cached DynamoDB data over plaintext, exposing it to in-VPC network interception.
rules.title: Require TLS on DAX cluster endpoints
requiredActions: Set cluster_endpoint_encryption_type = TLS

rules.threat: An event data store without termination protection can be deleted by an attacker to destroy the forensic record of their activity.
rules.title: Enable termination protection on CloudTrail event data stores
requiredActions: Set termination_protection_enabled = true

rules.threat: An unencrypted event data store leaves captured management/data events readable to anyone with bucket-level access, exposing sensitive API call records.
rules.title: Encrypt CloudTrail event data stores with a CMK

remediate.snippet: Set kms_key_id to a customer-managed KMS key ARN on the event data store.

requiredActions: Set kms_key_id to a CMK ARN

rules.threat: A QLDB ledger in ALLOW_ALL permissions mode ignores fine-grained IAM, letting any principal with ledger access read and write all tables.
rules.title: Use STANDARD permissions mode on QLDB ledgers
requiredActions: Set permissions_mode = STANDARD

rules.threat: An Athena database without encryption_configuration stores query metadata and CTAS output unencrypted, exposing result data in the backing S3 location.
rules.title: Encrypt Athena database storage

remediate.snippet: Add encryption_configuration { encryption_option = "SSE_KMS" kms_key = ... }.

requiredActions: Add an encryption_configuration block with SSE_KMS

rules.threat: A subnet that auto-assigns public IPs on launch makes every instance internet-reachable by default, defeating private-subnet intent.
rules.title: Disable auto-assign public IP on subnets
requiredActions: Set map_public_ip_on_launch = false

rules.threat: A Neptune cluster without deletion protection can be destroyed by a compromised principal, causing irreversible loss of graph data.
rules.title: Enable deletion protection on Neptune clusters
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true

rules.threat: A Glue dev endpoint without a security configuration runs notebooks/jobs with unencrypted job bookmarks and CloudWatch logs, exposing data and code.
rules.title: Attach a security configuration to Glue dev endpoints

remediate.snippet: Set security_configuration to a Glue security configuration enforcing CloudWatch, job bookmark, and S3 encryption.

requiredActions: Set security_configuration on the Glue dev endpoint

rules.threat: A SageMaker feature group online store without a KMS key stores feature data under an AWS-owned key, reducing control over potentially sensitive PII features.
rules.title: Encrypt SageMaker feature group online store with a CMK

remediate.snippet: Add online_store_config { security_config { kms_key_id = ... } } (the kms_key_id attribute).

requiredActions: Set kms_key_id in the online store security config

rules.threat: An EFS file system using the default service key instead of a CMK weakens key isolation and rotation control over data at rest.
rules.title: Encrypt EFS file systems with a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Set encrypted = true and kms_key_id to a customer-managed KMS key ARN.

requiredActions: Set kms_key_id to a CMK ARN and encrypted = true

rules.threat: An SQS queue using SSE-SQS (or none) instead of a CMK limits encryption control; without any SSE, messages sit unencrypted at rest exposing payload data.
rules.title: Encrypt SQS queues with a KMS CMK

remediate.snippet: Set kms_master_key_id to a CMK key id/alias on the queue.

requiredActions: Set kms_master_key_id on the SQS queue

rules.threat: An EBS volume using the default AWS-managed key instead of a CMK limits key isolation and rotation control over block-storage data at rest.
rules.title: Encrypt EBS volumes with a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Set encrypted = true and kms_key_id to a CMK ARN on the volume.

requiredActions: Set kms_key_id to a CMK ARN and encrypted = true

rules.threat: A MemoryDB cluster with TLS disabled transmits cached data and auth in plaintext within the VPC, exposing it to interception.
rules.title: Enable TLS on MemoryDB clusters
requiredActions: Set tls_enabled = true

rules.threat: An API Gateway stage with caching enabled but unencrypted stores potentially sensitive responses in plaintext in the managed cache.
rules.title: Encrypt API Gateway stage cache data

remediate.snippet: When cache_cluster_enabled = true, set cache_data_encrypted = true in method_settings.

requiredActions: Set cache_data_encrypted = true for cached methods

rules.threat: Neptune snapshots without copied tags lose data-classification and ownership context, breaking tag-based access controls on restored graph data.
rules.title: Copy tags to snapshots on Neptune clusters
requiredActions: Set copy_tags_to_snapshot = true

rules.threat: An Aurora cluster pinned to the legacy rds-ca-2019 certificate authority will hit expiry and may fall back to weaker validation, risking connection trust failures.
rules.title: Avoid the legacy CA certificate on Aurora clusters
requiredActions: Update ca_certificate_identifier to a current CA

rules.threat: A SageMaker notebook instance allowing IMDSv1 exposes its execution-role credentials to SSRF, granting an attacker the notebook's IAM permissions.
rules.title: Require IMDSv2 on SageMaker notebook instances

remediate.snippet: Add instance_metadata_service_configuration { minimum_instance_metadata_service_version = "2" }.

requiredActions: Set minimum_instance_metadata_service_version = 2

rules.threat: An MSK cluster at DEFAULT monitoring lacks per-broker and per-topic metrics needed to spot abnormal consumer/producer behavior indicative of compromise.
rules.title: Enable enhanced monitoring on MSK clusters
requiredActions: Set enhanced_monitoring above DEFAULT

rules.threat: A multi-node Redis replication group without automatic failover risks prolonged outage on primary failure, an availability weakness exploitable via targeted DoS.
rules.title: Enable automatic failover on Redis replication groups
requiredActions: Set automatic_failover_enabled = true

rules.threat: A publicly accessible DMS replication instance exposes the migration pipeline endpoint to the internet, providing a path to intercept or inject migrated data.
rules.title: Disable public access on DMS replication instances
requiredActions: Set publicly_accessible = false

rules.threat: A launch template with unencrypted block device mappings propagates unencrypted EBS volumes to every instance it launches, exposing data at rest at scale.
rules.title: Encrypt EBS volumes in launch templates

remediate.snippet: In block_device_mappings { ebs { encrypted = true kms_key_id = ... } } enable encryption.

requiredActions: Set encrypted = true in the launch template EBS block

rules.threat: Without X-Ray tracing on the GraphQL API, an attacker abusing resolvers leaves no distributed trace, hiding injection and data-exfiltration paths from responders.
rules.title: Enable X-Ray tracing on AppSync GraphQL APIs
requiredActions: Set xray_enabled = true on the aws_appsync_graphql_api

rules.threat: Without X-Ray tracing on the state machine, an attacker manipulating workflow inputs evades detection because no distributed trace records the abnormal execution path.
rules.title: Enable X-Ray tracing on Step Functions state machines

remediate.snippet: Add tracing_configuration { enabled = true } to the state machine.

requiredActions: Add tracing_configuration { enabled = true } to the aws_sfn_state_machine

rules.threat: With the default execute-api endpoint enabled, an attacker bypasses your WAF-protected custom domain and hits the API directly over the unprotected AWS-managed hostname.
rules.title: Disable the default execute-api endpoint on HTTP APIs
requiredActions: Set disable_execute_api_endpoint = true on the aws_apigatewayv2_api

rules.threat: Without a customer-managed KMS key, ElastiCache Serverless data at rest is encrypted only with an AWS-owned key the tenant cannot audit or revoke, weakening blast-radius containment after key compromise.
rules.title: Encrypt ElastiCache Serverless cache with a CMK

remediate.snippet: Set kms_key_id to a customer-managed KMS key ARN on the aws_elasticache_serverless_cache.

requiredActions: Set kms_key_id on the aws_elasticache_serverless_cache to a CMK ARN

rules.threat: A wildcard Principal in a Glacier vault access policy lets any AWS account or anonymous caller retrieve or delete archived backups, defeating immutable retention.
rules.title: Forbid public access in Glacier vault policies

remediate.snippet: Replace "Principal": "*" with specific account ARNs in the access_policy document.

requiredActions: Remove wildcard Principal from the aws_glacier_vault access_policy and scope to specific principals

rules.threat: A Neptune instance with auto minor upgrades disabled runs unpatched engine builds, letting an attacker exploit known graph-engine CVEs for data theft.
rules.title: Enable auto minor version upgrades on Neptune instances
requiredActions: Set auto_minor_version_upgrade = true on the aws_neptune_cluster_instance

rules.threat: An OpenSearch domain accepting legacy TLS lets a network attacker downgrade the connection and intercept indexed data in transit.
rules.title: Enforce a modern TLS security policy on OpenSearch domains

remediate.snippet: In domain_endpoint_options, set tls_security_policy = "Policy-Min-TLS-1-2-PFS-2023-10".

requiredActions: Set tls_security_policy to a TLS 1.2+ policy in domain_endpoint_options of the aws_opensearch_domain

rules.threat: An Elasticsearch domain accepting legacy TLS lets a network attacker downgrade the session and read sensitive search data in transit.
rules.title: Enforce a modern TLS security policy on Elasticsearch domains

remediate.snippet: In domain_endpoint_options, set tls_security_policy = "Policy-Min-TLS-1-2-PFS-2023-10".

requiredActions: Set tls_security_policy to a TLS 1.2+ policy in domain_endpoint_options of the aws_elasticsearch_domain

rules.threat: An MSK cluster with public access enabled exposes Kafka brokers to the internet, letting an attacker connect and consume or inject topic data.
rules.title: Disable public access on MSK clusters

remediate.snippet: In broker_node_group_info.connectivity_info.public_access set type = "DISABLED".

requiredActions: Set connectivity_info.public_access.type = "DISABLED" on the aws_msk_cluster

rules.threat: A WorkSpaces root volume left unencrypted exposes the OS and cached credentials on underlying storage to an attacker with snapshot access.
rules.title: Enable WorkSpaces root volume encryption
requiredActions: Set root_volume_encryption_enabled = true on the aws_workspaces_workspace

rules.threat: A password policy not requiring symbols permits low-entropy passwords that fall quickly to brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks against console logins.
rules.title: Require symbols in the IAM account password policy
requiredActions: Set require_symbols = true on the aws_iam_account_password_policy

rules.threat: A password policy not requiring numbers permits weak passwords vulnerable to dictionary attacks against IAM console users.
rules.title: Require numbers in the IAM account password policy
requiredActions: Set require_numbers = true on the aws_iam_account_password_policy

rules.threat: A password policy with no maximum age lets compromised credentials remain valid indefinitely, extending an attacker's persistence after a leak.
rules.title: Set a maximum password age in the IAM password policy
requiredActions: Set max_password_age (e.g. 90) on the aws_iam_account_password_policy

rules.threat: A password policy not requiring uppercase characters reduces password entropy, easing offline cracking of leaked IAM credential hashes.
rules.title: Require uppercase characters in the IAM password policy
requiredActions: Set require_uppercase_characters = true on the aws_iam_account_password_policy

rules.threat: A password policy not requiring lowercase characters lowers entropy, aiding brute-force attacks against IAM console logins.
rules.title: Require lowercase characters in the IAM password policy
requiredActions: Set require_lowercase_characters = true on the aws_iam_account_password_policy

rules.threat: An Aurora cluster with an inline master_password leaks the credential into Terraform state, letting anyone with state access gain full database administrative control.
rules.title: Manage Aurora master password via Secrets Manager
requiredActions: Set manage_master_user_password = true on the aws_rds_cluster and remove inline master_password

rules.threat: An RDS instance with an inline master_password commits the credential to Terraform state; managing it via Secrets Manager removes the plaintext secret and enables rotation.
rules.title: Manage RDS master password via Secrets Manager
requiredActions: Set manage_master_user_password = true on the aws_db_instance to use Secrets Manager

rules.threat: A REST API resource policy with a wildcard Principal allows any AWS principal or anonymous caller to invoke private endpoints, exposing internal operations.
rules.title: Forbid wildcard Principal in API Gateway REST API policies

remediate.snippet: Replace the wildcard Principal in the policy with specific principals or add aws:SourceVpce conditions.

requiredActions: Remove wildcard Principal from the aws_api_gateway_rest_api policy or add restricting conditions

rules.threat: An S3 object uploaded with a public-read ACL exposes that file to the entire internet even when the bucket itself is private, leaking individual sensitive objects.
rules.title: Forbid public-read ACL on individual S3 objects

remediate.snippet: Remove the public-read/public-read-write ACL from the object; use a private ACL.

requiredActions: Remove the public-read ACL from the aws_s3_object

rules.threat: A custom API Gateway domain accepting TLS 1.0/1.1 lets a network attacker downgrade clients and intercept API traffic over weak cipher suites.
rules.title: Enforce TLS 1.2 on API Gateway custom domain names
requiredActions: Set security_policy = "TLS_1_2" on the aws_api_gateway_domain_name

rules.threat: An HTTP API custom domain accepting legacy TLS lets attackers force a downgrade and intercept API calls.
rules.title: Enforce TLS 1.2 on API Gateway v2 custom domains

remediate.snippet: In domain_name_configuration set security_policy = "TLS_1_2".

requiredActions: Set security_policy = "TLS_1_2" in domain_name_configuration of the aws_apigatewayv2_domain_name

rules.threat: Cleartext AWS access keys in EC2 user_data are readable by any principal with ec2:DescribeInstanceAttribute and via IMDS, giving an attacker on the instance immediate credential access.
rules.title: Forbid hardcoded AWS keys in EC2 user_data

remediate.snippet: Remove hardcoded AWS access keys from user_data; use an instance profile (IAM role) instead.

requiredActions: Remove hardcoded AWS access keys from the aws_instance user_data and use an instance profile

rules.threat: Hardcoded AWS keys in a launch template's user_data propagate to every instance launched, giving an attacker on any of them long-lived credentials.
rules.title: Forbid hardcoded AWS keys in launch template user_data

remediate.snippet: Remove hardcoded AWS access keys from user_data; attach an IAM instance profile instead.

requiredActions: Remove hardcoded AWS access keys from the aws_launch_template user_data and use an IAM instance profile

rules.threat: A secret version with an inline literal secret_string commits the secret value into Terraform state, letting anyone with state access read it in plaintext.
rules.title: Forbid hardcoded secret_string in secret versions

remediate.snippet: Reference a variable or data source instead of a literal; better, let the application populate the secret out of band.

requiredActions: Remove the literal secret_string from the aws_secretsmanager_secret_version

rules.threat: A Glue connection storing a database PASSWORD in plaintext connection_properties leaks the credential in the catalog, letting any catalog reader pivot to the backing data store.
rules.title: Forbid plaintext PASSWORD in Glue connection properties

remediate.snippet: Store the password in Secrets Manager and reference it, or enable catalog connection-password encryption; remove plaintext PASSWORD.

requiredActions: Remove plaintext PASSWORD from the aws_glue_connection connection_properties and use Secrets Manager
